cdasws.cdasws

Module for accessing the Coordinate Data Analysis System (CDAS) web services https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/.

Copyright © 2018-2026 United States Government as represented by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. No copyright is claimed in the United States under Title 17, U.S.Code. All Other Rights Reserved.

Notes
  • Due to rate limiting implemented by the CDAS web services, an attempt to make simultaneous requests from many threads is likely to actually reduce performance. At this time, it is best to make calls from five or fewer threads.
  • Since CDAS data has datetime values with a UTC timezone, all client provided datetime values should have a timezone of UTC. If a given value's timezone is not UTC, the value is adjusted to UTC. If a given value has no timezone (is naive), a UTC timezone is set.
   1#!/usr/bin/env python3
   2

  33
  34"""
  35Module for accessing the Coordinate Data Analysis System (CDAS)
  36web services <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/>.<br>
  37
  38Copyright &copy; 2018-2026 United States Government as represented by the
  39National Aeronautics and Space Administration. No copyright is claimed in
  40the United States under Title 17, U.S.Code. All Other Rights Reserved.
  41
  42Notes
  43-----
  44<ul>
  45  <li>Due to rate limiting implemented by the CDAS web services, an
  46      attempt to make simultaneous requests from many threads is likely
  47      to actually reduce performance.  At this time, it is best to make
  48      calls from five or fewer threads.</li>
  49  <li>Since CDAS data has datetime values with a UTC timezone, all
  50      client provided datetime values should have a timezone of UTC.
  51      If a given value's timezone is not UTC, the value is adjusted to
  52      UTC.  If a given value has no timezone (is naive), a UTC timezone
  53      is set.</li>
  54</ul>
  55"""
  56
  57
  58import sys
  59import os
  60import platform
  61import logging
  62import re
  63#from importlib.util import find_spec
  64import urllib.parse
  65from urllib.parse import urlparse
  66#import json
  67from operator import itemgetter
  68import time
  69from datetime import datetime, timedelta
  70import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
  71from tempfile import mkstemp
  72from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Tuple, Union
  73
  74import requests
  75#import dateutil.parser
  76
  77from cdasws.datarepresentation import DataRepresentation
  78from cdasws.datarequest import AudioRequest, DataRequest
  79from cdasws.datarequest import CdfFormat, CdfRequest, Compression
  80from cdasws.datarequest import ImageFormat, GraphOptions, GraphRequest
  81from cdasws.datarequest import TextFormat, TextRequest, ThumbnailRequest
  82from cdasws.timeinterval import TimeInterval
  83from cdasws.doi import get_doi_landing_page_url
  84from cdasws import __version__, RETRY_LIMIT, NAMESPACES as NS
  85
  86

  90#    SPDM_AVAILABLE = True
  91#else:
  92#    SPDM_AVAILABLE = False
  93# python < 3.4
  94try:
  95    import spacepy.datamodel as spdm    # type: ignore
  96    SPDM_AVAILABLE = True
  97except ImportError:
  98    SPDM_AVAILABLE = False

 100try:
 101    from cdflib.xarray import cdf_to_xarray
 102    import xarray as xr
 103    CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE = True
 104except ImportError:
 105    try:
 106        import cdflib as cdf
 107        import xarray as xr   # pylint: disable=ungrouped-imports
 108        CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE = True
 109        def cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=False, to_unixtime=False,
 110                          fillval_to_nan=False):
 111            """
 112            Reads a CDF into an xarray.dataset.  This function exists
 113            to provide compatility with cdflib >= 1.0.1 for older
 114            releases of cdflib.
 115
 116            Parameters:
 117            -----------
 118            filename
 119                The path to the CDF file to read.
 120            to_datetime
 121                Whether or not to convert CDF_EPOCH/EPOCH_16/TT2000 to
 122                datetime, or leave them as is.
 123            to_unixtime
 124                Whether or not to convert CDF_EPOCH/EPOCH_16/TT2000 to
 125                unixtime, or leave them as is.
 126            fillval_to_nan
 127                If True, any data values that match the FILLVAL
 128                attribute for a variable will be set to NaN.
 129
 130            Returns
 131            -------
 132            xarray.dataset
 133                An XArray Dataset object.
 134            """
 135            return cdf.cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=to_datetime, # pylint: disable=no-member
 136                                     to_unixtime=to_unixtime,
 137                                     fillval_to_nan=fillval_to_nan)
 138    except ImportError:
 139        CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE = False
 140
 141
 142try:
 143    import requests_cache
 144    CACHE_AVAILABLE = True
 145except ImportError:
 146    CACHE_AVAILABLE = False
 147
 148
 149def _get_data_progress(
 150        progress: float,
 151        msg: str,
 152        value: Dict) -> int:
 153    """
 154    A get_data progress callback which adjusts the progress value for
 155    the download portion of a larger operation and then calls the
 156    "real" progress callback function with this adjusted progress value.
 157
 158    Parameters
 159    ----------
 160    progress
 161        Measure of progress.
 162    msg
 163        Message describing progress of get_data call.
 164    value
 165        Dictionary containing the function to call and values for
 166        computing the adjusted progress value.
 167    Returns
 168    -------
 169    int
 170        Flag indicating whether to continue with getting the data.
 171        0 to continue. 1 to abort getting the data.
 172    """
 173    progress_callback = value.get('progressCallback', None)
 174    progress_user_value = value.get('progressUserValue', None)
 175    adjusted_progress = value['progressStart'] + \
 176                        value['progressFraction'] * progress
 177
 178    if progress_callback is not None:
 179
 180        return progress_callback(adjusted_progress, msg,
 181                                 progress_user_value)
 182    return 0
 183
 184
 185class NullAuth(requests.auth.AuthBase): # pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods
 186    """
 187    Authentication class used to cause requests to ignore any ~/.netrc
 188    file.  The CDAS web services do not support authentication and
 189    a cdaweb (ftps) entry will cause CdasWs requests to fail with
 190    a 401 error.  See <https://github.com/psf/requests/issues/2773>.
 191    """
 192    def __call__(self, r):
 193        return r
 194
 195
 196class CdasWs:
 197    """
 198    Class representing the web service interface to NASA's
 199    Coordinated Data Analysis System (CDAS)
 200    <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov>.
 201
 202    Notes
 203    -----
 204    The logger used by this class has the class' name (CdasWs).  By default,
 205    it is configured with a NullHandler.  Users of this class may configure
 206    the logger to aid in diagnosing problems.
 207    """
 208    # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes
 209    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
 210    def __init__(
 211            self,
 212            endpoint=None,
 213            timeout=None,
 214            proxy=None,
 215            ca_certs=None,
 216            disable_ssl_certificate_validation=False,
 217            user_agent=None,
 218            disable_cache=False):
 219        """
 220        Creates an object representing the CDAS web services.
 221
 222        Parameters
 223        ----------
 224        endpoint
 225            URL of the CDAS web service.  If None, the default is
 226            'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'.
 227        timeout
 228            Number of seconds to wait for a response from the server.
 229        proxy
 230            HTTP proxy information.  For example,<pre>
 231            proxies = {
 232              'http': 'http://10.10.1.10:3128',
 233              'https': 'http://10.10.1.10:1080',
 234            }</pre>
 235            Proxy information can also be set with environment variables.
 236            For example,<pre>
 237            $ export HTTP_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:3128"
 238            $ export HTTPS_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:1080"</pre>
 239        ca_certs
 240            Path to certificate authority (CA) certificates that will
 241            override the default bundle.
 242        disable_ssl_certificate_validation
 243            Flag indicating whether to validate the SSL certificate.
 244        user_agent
 245            A value that is appended to the HTTP User-Agent value.
 246        disable_cache
 247            Flag indicating whether to disable HTTP caching.
 248        """
 249
 250        self.logger = logging.getLogger(type(self).__name__)
 251        self.logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
 252
 253        self.logger.debug('endpoint = %s', endpoint)
 254        self.logger.debug('ca_certs = %s', ca_certs)
 255        self.logger.debug('disable_ssl_certificate_validation = %s',
 256                          disable_ssl_certificate_validation)
 257        self.logger.debug('disable_cache = %s', disable_cache)
 258
 259        if endpoint is None:
 260            self._endpoint = 'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'
 261        else:
 262            self._endpoint = endpoint
 263
 264        self._user_agent = 'cdasws/' + __version__ + ' (' + \
 265            platform.python_implementation() + ' ' \
 266            + platform.python_version() + '; ' + platform.platform() + ')'
 267
 268        if user_agent is not None:
 269            self._user_agent += ' (' + user_agent + ')'
 270
 271        self._request_headers = {
 272            #'Content-Type' : 'application/json',
 273            'Content-Type' : 'application/xml',
 274            'Accept' : 'application/xml',
 275            'User-Agent' : self._user_agent,
 276            #'Accept-Encoding' : 'gzip'  # only beneficial for icdfml responses
 277        }
 278        if CACHE_AVAILABLE and disable_cache is not True:
 279            self._session = requests_cache.CachedSession('cdasws_cache',
 280                                                         cache_control=True)
 281        else:
 282            self._session = requests.Session()
 283
 284        self._session.headers.update(self._request_headers)
 285        self._session.auth = NullAuth()
 286
 287        if ca_certs is not None:
 288            self._session.verify = ca_certs
 289
 290        if disable_ssl_certificate_validation is True:
 291            self._session.verify = False
 292
 293        if proxy is not None:
 294            self._proxy = proxy
 295
 296        self._timeout = timeout
 297
 298        endpoint_components = urlparse(self._endpoint)
 299        self._hdp_registry = endpoint_components.scheme + '://' + \
 300            endpoint_components.netloc + '/registry/hdp/SscId.xql'
 301
 302    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
 303
 304
 305    def __del__(self):
 306        """
 307        Destructor.  Closes all network connections.
 308        """
 309
 310        self.close()
 311
 312
 313    def close(self) -> None:
 314        """
 315        Closes any persistent network connections.  Generally, deleting
 316        this object is sufficient and calling this method is unnecessary.
 317        """
 318        self._session.close()
 319
 320
 321    def get_observatory_groups(
 322            self,
 323            **keywords: str
 324        ) -> List[Dict]:
 325        """
 326        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups from the server.
 327
 328        Parameters
 329        ----------
 330        keywords
 331            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 332            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 333            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting
 334            this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 335            based upon their instrumentType value.
 336        Returns
 337        -------
 338        List
 339            An array of ObservatoryGroupDescription
 340            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 341            ObservatoryGroupDescription in
 342            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 343        """
 344        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 345            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups?instrumentType=' + \
 346                      urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 347        else:
 348            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups'
 349
 350        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 351
 352        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 353
 354        if response.status_code != 200:
 355
 356            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 357                             response.status_code)
 358            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 359            return []
 360
 361        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 362            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 363
 364        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 365
 366        observatory_group_descriptions = []
 367        for description in observatory_response.findall(\
 368            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupDescription', namespaces=NS):
 369
 370            observatory_ids = []
 371            for observatory_id in description.findall(\
 372                'cdas:ObservatoryId', namespaces=NS):
 373
 374                observatory_ids.append(observatory_id.text)
 375
 376            observatory_group_descriptions.append({
 377                'Name': description.find(\
 378                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 379                'ObservatoryId': observatory_ids
 380            })
 381
 382        return observatory_group_descriptions
 383
 384
 385    def get_instrument_types(
 386            self,
 387            **keywords: str
 388        ) -> List[Dict]:
 389        """
 390        Gets descriptions of the instrument types from the server.
 391
 392        Parameters
 393        ----------
 394        keywords
 395            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 396            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
 397            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
 398            indicates that no instrumentTypes are eliminated based upon
 399            their observatory value.<br>
 400            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from
 401            those returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting
 402            this parameter indicates that no instrumentTypes are
 403            eliminated based upon their observatoryGroup value.</br>
 404        Returns
 405        -------
 406        List
 407            An array of InstrumentTypeDescription
 408            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 409            InstrumentTypeDescription in
 410            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 411        """
 412        if 'observatory' in keywords:
 413            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatory=' \
 414                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
 415        elif 'observatoryGroup' in keywords:
 416            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatoryGroup=' \
 417                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatoryGroup'])
 418        else:
 419            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes'
 420
 421        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 422
 423        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 424
 425        if response.status_code != 200:
 426
 427            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 428                             response.status_code)
 429            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 430            return []
 431
 432        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 433            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 434
 435        instrument_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 436
 437        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 438            self.logger.debug('instrument_response = %s',
 439                              ET.tostring(instrument_response))
 440
 441        instrument_types = []
 442        for description in instrument_response.findall(\
 443            'cdas:InstrumentTypeDescription', namespaces=NS):
 444
 445            instrument_types.append({
 446                'Name': description.find('cdas:Name',
 447                                         namespaces=NS).text
 448                })
 449        return instrument_types
 450
 451
 452    def get_instruments(
 453            self,
 454            **keywords: str
 455        ) -> List[Dict]:
 456        """
 457        Gets descriptions of the instruments from the server.
 458
 459        Parameters
 460        ----------
 461        keywords
 462            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 463            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
 464            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
 465            indicates that no instruments are eliminated based upon their
 466            observatory value.<br>
 467            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 468            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 469            parameter indicates that no instruments are eliminated based
 470            upon their instrument type.<br>
 471        Returns
 472        -------
 473        List
 474            An array of InstrumentDescription
 475            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 476            InstrumentDescription in
 477            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 478        """
 479        if 'observatory' in keywords:
 480            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?observatory=' \
 481                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
 482        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 483            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?instrumentType=' \
 484                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 485        else:
 486            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments'
 487
 488        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 489
 490        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 491
 492        if response.status_code != 200:
 493
 494            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 495                             response.status_code)
 496            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 497            return []
 498
 499        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 500            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 501
 502        instruments_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 503
 504        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 505            self.logger.debug('instruments = %s', response.text)
 506                              #ET.indent(instruments_response, space='    '))
 507
 508        instruments = []
 509        for instrument_description in instruments_response.findall(\
 510            'cdas:InstrumentDescription', namespaces=NS):
 511
 512            instruments.append({
 513                'Name': instrument_description.find(\
 514                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 515                'ShortDescription': instrument_description.find(\
 516                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
 517                'LongDescription': instrument_description.find(\
 518                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
 519            })
 520
 521        return instruments
 522
 523
 524    def get_observatories(
 525            self,
 526            **keywords: str
 527        ) -> List[Dict]:
 528        """
 529        Gets descriptions of the observatories from the server.
 530
 531        Parameters
 532        ----------
 533        keywords
 534            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 535            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned
 536            by `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter
 537            indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon
 538            their instrument value.<br>
 539            <b>instrumentType</b> - in instrument type value from those
 540            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 541            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 542            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 543        Returns
 544        -------
 545        List
 546            An array of ObservatoryDescriptions
 547            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 548            ObservatoryDescription in
 549            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 550        """
 551        if 'instrument' in keywords:
 552            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrument=' \
 553                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrument'])
 554        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 555            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrumentType=' \
 556                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 557        else:
 558            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories'
 559
 560        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 561
 562        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 563
 564        if response.status_code != 200:
 565
 566            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 567                             response.status_code)
 568            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 569            return []
 570
 571        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 572            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 573
 574        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 575
 576        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 577            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
 578
 579        observatories = []
 580
 581        for observatory in observatory_response.findall(\
 582            'cdas:ObservatoryDescription', namespaces=NS):
 583            observatories.append({
 584                'Name': observatory.find(\
 585                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 586                'ShortDescription': observatory.find(\
 587                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
 588                'LongDescription': observatory.find(\
 589                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
 590            })
 591
 592        return observatories
 593
 594
 595    def get_observatory_groups_and_instruments(
 596            self,
 597            **keywords: str
 598        ) -> List[Dict]:
 599        """
 600        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups (and associated
 601        instruments) from the server.
 602
 603        Parameters
 604        ----------
 605        keywords
 606            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 607            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 608            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 609            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 610            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 611        Returns
 612        -------
 613        List
 614            An array of ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription
 615            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 616            ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription in
 617            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 618        """
 619        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 620            url = self._endpoint \
 621                  + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments?instrumentType=' \
 622                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 623        else:
 624            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments'
 625
 626        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 627
 628        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 629
 630        if response.status_code != 200:
 631
 632            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 633                             response.status_code)
 634            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 635            return []
 636
 637        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 638            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 639
 640        observatories_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 641
 642        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 643            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
 644
 645        o_g_i_ds = []
 646
 647        for o_g_i_d in observatories_response.findall(\
 648            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription',\
 649            namespaces=NS):
 650
 651            o_g_i_d_name = o_g_i_d.find('cdas:Name',
 652                                        namespaces=NS).text
 653            o_is = []
 654            for o_i in o_g_i_d.findall('cdas:ObservatoryInstruments',
 655                                       namespaces=NS):
 656
 657                o_i_name = o_i.find('cdas:Name',
 658                                    namespaces=NS).text
 659                i_ds = []
 660                for i_d in o_i.findall('cdas:InstrumentDescription',
 661                                       namespaces=NS):
 662                    i_d_name = i_d.find('cdas:Name',
 663                                        namespaces=NS).text
 664                    i_d_short_description = \
 665                        i_d.find('cdas:ShortDescription',
 666                                 namespaces=NS).text
 667                    i_d_long_description = \
 668                         i_d.find('cdas:LongDescription',
 669                                  namespaces=NS).text
 670                    i_ds.append({
 671                        'Name': i_d_name,
 672                        'ShortDescription': i_d_short_description,
 673                        'LongDescription': i_d_long_description
 674                    })
 675                o_is.append({
 676                    'Name': o_i_name,
 677                    'InstrumentDescription': i_ds
 678                })
 679
 680            o_g_i_ds.append({
 681                'Name': o_g_i_d_name,
 682                'ObservatoryInstruments': o_is
 683            })
 684
 685        return o_g_i_ds
 686
 687
 688    # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
 689    def get_datasets(
 690            self,
 691            **keywords: str
 692        ) -> List[Dict]:
 693        """
 694        Gets descriptions of the specified datasets from the server.
 695
 696        Parameters
 697        ----------
 698        keywords
 699            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 700            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from those
 701            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting this
 702            parameter
 703            indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their
 704            observatoryGroup value.<br>
 705            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 706            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 707            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
 708            upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 709            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory name value from those
 710            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this
 711            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
 712            upon their observatory value.<br>
 713            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned by
 714            `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter indicates
 715            that no datasets are eliminated based upon their instrument
 716            value.<br>
 717            <b>startDate</b> - a datetime specifying the start of a time
 718            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
 719            parameter is ommited, the time interval will begin infinitely
 720            in the past.<br>
 721            <b>stopDate</b> - a datetime specifying the end of a time
 722            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
 723            parameter is omitted, the time interval will end infinitely
 724            in the future.<br>
 725            <b>id</b> - a dataset identifier. The value may be a CDAS
 726            (e.g., AC_H2_MFI), DOI (e.g., 10.48322/fh85-fj47), or SPASE
 727            [ResourceID] (e.g., spase://NASA/NumericalData/ACE/MAG/L2/PT1H)
 728            identifier. If specified, all other keywords are ignored.<br>
 729            <b>idPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 730            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS identifier value.
 731            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.<br>
 732            <b>labelPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 733            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS label text.
 734            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
 735            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?i)` for case insensitive
 736            match mode) are supported and likely to be useful in this
 737            case.<br>
 738            <b>notesPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 739            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS notes text.
 740            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
 741            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?s)` for dotall match mode)
 742            are supported and likely to be useful in this case.<br>
 743        Returns
 744        -------
 745        List
 746            A list of dictionaries containing descriptions of the datasets
 747            requested.  The dictionary structure is defined by the
 748            DatasetDescription element in
 749            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 750        """
 751        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets?'
 752
 753        observatory_groups = keywords.get('observatoryGroup', None)
 754        if observatory_groups is not None:
 755            if isinstance(observatory_groups, str):
 756                observatory_groups = [observatory_groups]
 757            for observatory_group in observatory_groups:
 758                url = url + 'observatoryGroup=' \
 759                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory_group) + '&'
 760
 761        instrument_types = keywords.get('instrumentType', None)
 762        if instrument_types is not None:
 763            if isinstance(instrument_types, str):
 764                instrument_types = [instrument_types]
 765            for instrument_type in instrument_types:
 766                url = url + 'instrumentType=' \
 767                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument_type) + '&'
 768
 769        observatories = keywords.get('observatory', None)
 770        if observatories is not None:
 771            if isinstance(observatories, str):
 772                observatories = [observatories]
 773            for observatory in observatories:
 774                url = url + 'observatory=' \
 775                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory) + '&'
 776
 777        instruments = keywords.get('instrument', None)
 778        if instruments is not None:
 779            if isinstance(instruments, str):
 780                instruments = [instruments]
 781            for instrument in instruments:
 782                url = url + 'instrument=' \
 783                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument) + '&'
 784
 785        if 'startDate' in keywords:
 786            url = url + 'startDate=' \
 787                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['startDate']) + '&'
 788
 789        if 'stopDate' in keywords:
 790            url = url + 'stopDate=' \
 791                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['stopDate']) + '&'
 792
 793        if 'id' in keywords:
 794            url = url + 'id=' \
 795                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['id']) + '&'
 796
 797        if 'idPattern' in keywords:
 798            url = url + 'idPattern=' \
 799                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['idPattern']) + '&'
 800
 801        if 'labelPattern' in keywords:
 802            url = url + 'labelPattern=' \
 803                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['labelPattern']) + '&'
 804
 805        if 'notesPattern' in keywords:
 806            url = url + 'notesPattern=' \
 807                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['notesPattern']) + '&'
 808
 809        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url[:-1])
 810
 811        response = self._session.get(url[:-1], timeout=self._timeout)
 812
 813        if response.status_code != 200:
 814
 815            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 816                             response.status_code)
 817            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 818            return []
 819
 820        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 821            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 822
 823        dss = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 824
 825        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 826            self.logger.debug('datasets = %s', response.text)
 827
 828        datasets = []
 829        for ds in dss.findall('cdas:DatasetDescription',
 830                              namespaces=NS):
 831
 832            observatory_groups = []
 833            for o_g in ds.findall('cdas:ObservatoryGroup',
 834                                  namespaces=NS):
 835                observatory_groups.append(o_g.text)
 836
 837            instrument_types = []
 838            for i_t in ds.findall('cdas:InstrumentType',
 839                                  namespaces=NS):
 840                instrument_types.append(i_t.text)
 841
 842            dataset_links = []
 843            for d_l in ds.findall('cdas:DatasetLink',
 844                                  namespaces=NS):
 845                dataset_links.append({
 846                    'Title': d_l.find('cdas:Title',
 847                                      namespaces=NS).text,
 848                    'Text': d_l.find('cdas:Text',
 849                                     namespaces=NS).text,
 850                    'Url': d_l.find('cdas:Url',
 851                                    namespaces=NS).text,
 852                })
 853
 854            observatories = []
 855            for obs_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Observatory',
 856                                       namespaces=NS):
 857                observatories.append(obs_elem.text)
 858
 859            instruments = []
 860            for instr_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Instrument',
 861                                         namespaces=NS):
 862                instruments.append(instr_elem.text)
 863
 864            dataset = {
 865                'Id': ds.find('cdas:Id', namespaces=NS).text,
 866                'Observatory': observatories,
 867                'Instrument': instruments,
 868                'ObservatoryGroup': observatory_groups,
 869                'InstrumentType': instrument_types,
 870                'Label': ds.find('cdas:Label',
 871                                 namespaces=NS).text,
 872                'TimeInterval': {
 873                    'Start': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:Start',
 874                                     namespaces=NS).text,
 875                    'End': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:End',
 876                                   namespaces=NS).text
 877                },
 878                'PiName': ds.find('cdas:PiName',
 879                                  namespaces=NS).text,
 880                'PiAffiliation': ds.find('cdas:PiAffiliation',
 881                                         namespaces=NS).text,
 882                'Notes': ds.find('cdas:Notes',
 883                                 namespaces=NS).text,
 884                'DatasetLink': dataset_links
 885            }
 886            doi = ds.find('cdas:Doi', namespaces=NS)
 887            if doi is not None:
 888                dataset['Doi'] = doi.text
 889
 890            spase_resource_id = ds.find('cdas:SpaseResourceId',
 891                                        namespaces=NS)
 892            if spase_resource_id is not None:
 893                dataset['SpaseResourceId'] = spase_resource_id.text
 894
 895            additional_metadata = []
 896            for add_meta in ds.findall('cdas:AdditionalMetadata',
 897                                       namespaces=NS):
 898                meta_type = add_meta.attrib['Type']
 899                value = add_meta.text
 900                additional_metadata.append({
 901                    'Type': meta_type,
 902                    'value': value
 903                })
 904
 905            if len(additional_metadata) > 0:
 906                dataset['AdditionalMetadata'] = additional_metadata
 907
 908            datasets.append(dataset)
 909
 910        return sorted(datasets, key=itemgetter('Id'))
 911    # pylint: enable=too-many-branches
 912
 913
 914    def get_client_library_example(
 915            self,
 916            dataset: str,
 917            language: str = 'Python'
 918        ) -> str:
 919        """
 920        Gets client library example code for the given dataset and
 921        programming language.
 922
 923        Parameters
 924        ----------
 925        dataset
 926            The dataset to use in the example code.  It must be an Id
 927            returned by get_datasets.
 928        language
 929            The programming language of the example code.  It must be
 930            either Python or IDL.
 931
 932        Returns
 933        -------
 934        str
 935            Requested client library example code.
 936        """
 937        library = 'cdaswsPy'
 938        if language.lower() == 'idl':
 939            library = 'cdaswsIdl'
 940
 941        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
 942                dataset + '/clientLibraryExample/' + library
 943
 944        # Do not use session headers (Accept: Application/xml)
 945        response = requests.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 946
 947        if response.status_code != 200:
 948            self.logger.warning('%s failed with status %d', response.url,
 949                                response.status_code)
 950            return None
 951
 952        return response.text
 953
 954
 955    @staticmethod
 956    def get_doi_landing_page_url(
 957            doi: str
 958        ) -> str:
 959        """
 960        Returns a URL to the given Digital Object Identifier's landing
 961        page (metadata for the DOI).
 962
 963        Note: this method is deprecated.  You should call 
 964        doi.get_doi_landing_page_url directly.
 965
 966        Parameters
 967        ----------
 968        doi
 969            digital object identifier.
 970        Returns
 971        -------
 972        str
 973            A URL to the DOI's landing page.
 974        """
 975
 976        return get_doi_landing_page_url(doi)
 977
 978
 979    @staticmethod
 980    def get_citation(
 981            doi: str
 982        ) -> str:
 983        """
 984        Returns the citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 985
 986        Parameters
 987        ----------
 988        doi
 989            digital object identifier.
 990        Returns
 991        -------
 992        str
 993            The citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 994        """
 995
 996        url = 'https://doi.org/' + doi
 997        headers = {'Accept': 'text/x-bibliography; style=apa'}
 998        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers,
 999                                timeout=30)
1000
1001        return response.text
1002
1003
1004    def get_inventory(
1005            self,
1006            identifier: str,
1007            **keywords: str
1008        ) -> List[TimeInterval]:
1009        """
1010        Gets a description of the specified dataset's data inventory.
1011
1012        Parameters
1013        ----------
1014        identifier
1015            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1016        keywords
1017            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1018            <b>timeInterval</b> - `timeinterval.TimeInterval` to restrict
1019            returned inventory.
1020        Returns
1021        -------
1022        List
1023            An array of `timeinterval.TimeInterval`s when data is
1024            available.
1025        """
1026
1027        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1028                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/inventory'
1029
1030        if 'timeInterval' in keywords:
1031            time_interval_keyword = keywords['timeInterval']
1032            url = url + '/' + \
1033                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.start) + \
1034                  ',' + \
1035                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.end)
1036
1037        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
1038
1039        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1040
1041        if response.status_code != 200:
1042
1043            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1044                             response.status_code)
1045            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1046            return []
1047
1048        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1049            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1050
1051        inventory = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1052        intervals = []
1053        for inventory_desc in inventory.findall(\
1054                                  'cdas:InventoryDescription',
1055                                  namespaces=NS):
1056            for time_interval in inventory_desc.findall(\
1057                                     'cdas:TimeInterval',
1058                                     namespaces=NS):
1059                intervals.append(
1060                    TimeInterval(
1061                        time_interval.find('cdas:Start',
1062                                           namespaces=NS).text,
1063                        time_interval.find('cdas:End',
1064                                           namespaces=NS).text
1065                    )
1066                )
1067
1068        return intervals
1069
1070
1071    def get_example_time_interval(
1072            self,
1073            identifier: str,
1074        ) -> TimeInterval:
1075        """
1076        Gets a small example time interval for the specified dataset.  The
1077        interval is near the end of the dataset's data inventory.  The
1078        returned interval is not guaranteed to have non-fill data for any
1079        specific variable.
1080
1081        Parameters
1082        ----------
1083        identifier
1084            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1085        Returns
1086        -------
1087        timeinterval.TimeInterval
1088            An small example time interval that is likely, but not
1089            guaranteed, to have data or None if an interval cannot be
1090            found.
1091        """
1092
1093        time_intervals = self.get_inventory(identifier)
1094        if len(time_intervals) < 1:
1095            return None
1096        example_interval = time_intervals[-1]
1097        if re.search('MMS[1-4]_.+_BRST_.+', identifier):
1098            time_delta = timedelta(seconds=1)
1099        else:
1100            time_delta = timedelta(hours=2)
1101        example_interval.start = example_interval.end - time_delta
1102        return example_interval
1103
1104
1105    def get_variables(
1106            self,
1107            identifier: str
1108        ) -> List[Dict]:
1109        """
1110        Gets a description of the variables in the specified dataset.
1111
1112        Parameters
1113        ----------
1114        identifier
1115            dataset identifier of data to get.
1116        Returns
1117        -------
1118        List
1119            A List of dictionary descriptions of the variables in
1120            the specified dataset.  The dictionary structure is defined by
1121            the VariableDescription element in
1122            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
1123        """
1124
1125        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1126                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/variables'
1127
1128        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1129
1130        if response.status_code != 200:
1131
1132            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1133                             response.status_code)
1134            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1135            return []
1136
1137        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1138            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1139
1140        var_descriptions = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1141
1142        variables = []
1143        for var_description in var_descriptions.findall(\
1144                                   'cdas:VariableDescription',
1145                                   namespaces=NS):
1146            name = var_description.find('cdas:Name',
1147                                        namespaces=NS).text
1148            short_description = var_description.find(\
1149                                        'cdas:ShortDescription',
1150                                        namespaces=NS).text
1151            if short_description is None:
1152                short_description = ''
1153
1154            long_description = var_description.find(\
1155                                        'cdas:LongDescription',
1156                                        namespaces=NS).text
1157            if long_description is None:
1158                long_description = ''
1159
1160            variables.append({
1161                'Name': name,
1162                'ShortDescription': short_description,
1163                'LongDescription': long_description
1164            })
1165
1166        return variables
1167
1168
1169    def get_variable_names(
1170            self,
1171            identifier: str
1172        ) -> List[str]:
1173        """
1174        Gets the names of the variables in the specified dataset.  This
1175        method is like the get_variables method except that it only returns
1176        the variable names and not the other metadata.
1177
1178        Parameters
1179        ----------
1180        identifier
1181            dataset identifier of data to get.
1182        Returns
1183        -------
1184        List
1185            A List of the names of the variables in the specified dataset.
1186        """
1187
1188        variable_names = []
1189        for variable in self.get_variables(identifier):
1190            variable_names.append(variable['Name'])
1191
1192        return variable_names
1193
1194
1195    @staticmethod
1196    def _get_thumbnail_description_dict(
1197            file_description_elem: ET.Element
1198        ) -> Dict:
1199        """
1200        Gets ThumbnailDescription dictionary representation from the
1201        given FileDescription element.
1202
1203        Parameters
1204        ----------
1205        file_description_elem
1206            a FileDescription Element.
1207        Returns
1208        -------
1209        Dict
1210            a Dictionary representation of the ThumbnailDescription
1211            contained in the given FileDescription element.
1212        """
1213        thumbnail_desc = file_description_elem.find(\
1214                             'cdas:ThumbnailDescription',
1215                             namespaces=NS)
1216        if thumbnail_desc is not None:
1217            time_interval = thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:TimeInterval',
1218                                                namespaces=NS)
1219            start = time_interval.find('cdas:Start',
1220                                       namespaces=NS).text
1221            end = time_interval.find('cdas:End',
1222                                     namespaces=NS).text
1223            return {
1224                'Name': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:Name',
1225                                            namespaces=NS).text,
1226                'Dataset': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:Dataset',
1227                                               namespaces=NS).text,
1228                'TimeInterval': {
1229                    'Start': start,
1230                    'End': end
1231                },
1232                'VarName': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:VarName',
1233                                               namespaces=NS).text,
1234                'Options': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1235                                   'cdas:Options',
1236                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1237                'NumFrames': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1238                                     'cdas:NumFrames',
1239                                     namespaces=NS).text),
1240                'NumRows': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1241                                   'cdas:NumRows',
1242                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1243                'NumCols': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1244                                   'cdas:NumCols',
1245                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1246                'TitleHeight': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1247                                       'cdas:TitleHeight',
1248                                       namespaces=NS).text),
1249                'ThumbnailHeight': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1250                                           'cdas:ThumbnailHeight',
1251                                           namespaces=NS).text),
1252                'ThumbnailWidth': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1253                                           'cdas:ThumbnailWidth',
1254                                           namespaces=NS).text),
1255                'StartRecord': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1256                                       'cdas:StartRecord',
1257                                       namespaces=NS).text),
1258                'MyScale': float(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1259                                     'cdas:MyScale',
1260                                     namespaces=NS).text),
1261                'XyStep': float(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1262                                    'cdas:XyStep',
1263                                    namespaces=NS).text)
1264            }
1265        return None
1266
1267
1268    @staticmethod
1269    def _get_data_result_dict(
1270            xml_data_result: str
1271        ) -> Dict:
1272        """
1273        Gets DataResult dictionary representation from the
1274        given XML DataResult element.
1275
1276        Parameters
1277        ----------
1278        xml_data_result
1279            XML representation of a DataResult.
1280        Returns
1281        -------
1282        Dict
1283            a Dictionary representation of the given XML representation
1284            of a DataResult.
1285        """
1286        data_result = ET.fromstring(xml_data_result)
1287        file_descriptions = []
1288        for file_description in data_result.findall(\
1289            'cdas:FileDescription', namespaces=NS):
1290
1291            dict_file_description = {
1292                'Name': file_description.find('cdas:Name',
1293                                              namespaces=NS).text,
1294                'MimeType': file_description.find(\
1295                                'cdas:MimeType',
1296                                namespaces=NS).text,
1297                'StartTime': file_description.find(\
1298                                 'cdas:StartTime',
1299                                 namespaces=NS).text,
1300                'EndTime': file_description.find(\
1301                               'cdas:EndTime',
1302                               namespaces=NS).text,
1303                'Length': int(file_description.find(\
1304                                  'cdas:Length',
1305                                  namespaces=NS).text),
1306                'LastModified': file_description.find(\
1307                                    'cdas:LastModified',
1308                                    namespaces=NS).text
1309            }
1310            thumbnail_dict = CdasWs._get_thumbnail_description_dict(\
1311                                     file_description)
1312            if thumbnail_dict is not None:
1313                dict_file_description['ThumbnailDescription'] = \
1314                    thumbnail_dict
1315
1316            thumbnail_id_elem = file_description.find(\
1317                                    'cdas:ThumbnailId',
1318                                    namespaces=NS)
1319            if thumbnail_id_elem is not None:
1320                dict_file_description['ThumbnailId'] = \
1321                    thumbnail_id_elem.text
1322
1323            file_descriptions.append(dict_file_description)
1324
1325        if len(file_descriptions) > 0:
1326            return {
1327                'FileDescription': file_descriptions
1328            }
1329        return None
1330
1331
1332    def get_data_result(
1333            self,
1334            data_request: DataRequest,
1335            progress_callback: Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1336            progress_user_value: Any
1337        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1338        """
1339        Submits the given request to the server and returns the result.
1340        This is a relatively low-level method and most callers should
1341        probably use a higher-level method such as get_data.
1342
1343        Parameters
1344        ----------
1345        data_request
1346            data request.
1347        progress_callback
1348            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1349            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1350            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1351            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1352            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1353            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1354            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1355            the progress of this call.
1356        progressUserValue
1357            value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
1358        Returns
1359        -------
1360        Tuple
1361            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1362            successful.<br>
1363            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1364            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1365            or None.
1366        See Also
1367        --------
1368        CdasWs.get_data
1369        """
1370
1371        #self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.json())
1372        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.xml_str())
1373
1374        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets'
1375
1376        for retries in range(RETRY_LIMIT):
1377            #response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.json(),
1378            response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.xml_str(),
1379                                          timeout=self._timeout)
1380
1381            if response.status_code == 200:
1382
1383                data_result = CdasWs._get_data_result_dict(response.text)
1384
1385                if not data_result:
1386                    return (response.status_code, None)
1387
1388                return (response.status_code, data_result)
1389
1390            if response.status_code == 429 or \
1391               response.status_code == 503 and \
1392               'Retry-After' in response.headers:
1393
1394                retry_after = response.headers['Retry-After']
1395
1396                self.logger.debug('429/503 status with Retry-After header: %s',
1397                                  retry_after)
1398
1399                if progress_callback is not None:
1400                    if progress_callback(0.2, 'Waiting ' + retry_after + \
1401                                         's before making server request.',
1402                                         progress_user_value) != 0:
1403                        return (204, None)
1404
1405                retry_after = int(retry_after)
1406
1407                self.logger.info('Sleeping %d seconds before making request',
1408                                 retry_after)
1409                time.sleep(retry_after)
1410
1411            else:
1412                self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1413                                 response.status_code)
1414                self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1415                self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1416                return (response.status_code, None)
1417
1418        self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d after %d retries',
1419                         url, response.status_code, retries + 1)
1420        self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1421        self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1422        return (response.status_code, None)
1423
1424
1425    def get_data_file(
1426            self,
1427            dataset: str,
1428            variables: List[str],
1429            start: Union[datetime, str], end: Union[datetime, str],
1430            **keywords: Union[
1431                Dict,
1432                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1433                Any]
1434        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1435        """
1436        Gets the specified data file from the server.
1437
1438        Parameters
1439        ----------
1440        dataset
1441            dataset identifier of data to get.
1442        variables
1443            array containing names of variables to get.
1444        start
1445            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1446        end
1447            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1448        keywords
1449            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1450            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1451            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1452            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1453            may contain the following keys: interval,
1454            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1455            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1456            defaults.<br>
1457            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1458            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1459            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1460            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1461            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1462            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data_file()
1463            function will immediately return (204, None).  The float
1464            parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress
1465            and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1466            the progress of this call.<br>
1467            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1468            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1469        Returns
1470        -------
1471        Tuple
1472            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1473            successful.<br>
1474            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1475            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1476            or None.
1477        Raises
1478        ------
1479        ValueError
1480            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1481        See Also
1482        --------
1483        CdasWs.get_data : In addition to what get_data_file does,
1484            get_data also downloads and reads the data file into memory
1485            (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset object).
1486        """
1487        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1488        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1489        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1490        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1491
1492        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1493                                                                  end)
1494
1495        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1496                                  TimeInterval(start_datetime,
1497                                               end_datetime),
1498                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1499                                  **keywords.get('binData', {}))
1500
1501        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1502        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1503
1504        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1505
1506        if progress_callback is not None:
1507            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1508                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1509                return (204, None)
1510
1511        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1512                                                        progress_callback,
1513                                                        progress_user_value)
1514
1515        if progress_callback is not None:
1516            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Initial server request complete.',
1517                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1518                return (status_code, None)
1519
1520        return (status_code, data_result)
1521
1522
1523    def download(
1524            self,
1525            url: str,
1526            size: int = 0,
1527            **keywords
1528        ) -> str:
1529        """
1530        Downloads the file specified by the given URL to a temporary
1531        file without reading all of it into memory.  This method
1532        utilizes the connection pool and persistent HTTP connection
1533        to the CdasWs server.
1534
1535        Parameters
1536        ----------
1537        url
1538            URL of file to download.
1539        size
1540            number of bytes in file to download.
1541        keywords
1542            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1543            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1544            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1545            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1546            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1547            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1548            getting the data will be aborted and this download() function
1549            will immediately return None.  The float parameter
1550            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1551            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1552            the progress of this call.<br>
1553            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
1554            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1555        Returns
1556        -------
1557        str
1558            name of tempory file or None if there was an error.
1559        """
1560        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1561
1562        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1563        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1564
1565        suffix = os.path.splitext(urlparse(url).path)[1]
1566
1567        file_descriptor, tmp_filename = mkstemp(suffix=suffix)
1568
1569        download_bytes = 0
1570        next_progress_report = 0.1
1571        with self._session.get(url, stream=True,
1572                               timeout=self._timeout) as response:
1573
1574            file = open(tmp_filename, 'wb')
1575            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
1576                if chunk:  # filter out keep-alive new chunks
1577                    file.write(chunk)
1578                    # file.flush()
1579                    if progress_callback is not None:
1580                        download_bytes += len(chunk)
1581                        if size == 0:
1582                            download_progress = 0.0
1583                        else:
1584                            download_progress = float(download_bytes) / size
1585                        if download_progress > next_progress_report:
1586                            next_progress_report += download_progress
1587                            if progress_callback(download_progress,\
1588                                   'Continuing download of data.',
1589                                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1590
1591                                file.close()
1592                                os.close(file_descriptor)
1593                                return None
1594            file.close()
1595            os.close(file_descriptor)
1596
1597        if progress_callback is not None:
1598            if progress_callback(0.4,
1599                                 'Data download complete. Reading data.',
1600                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1601                return None
1602
1603        return tmp_filename
1604
1605
1606    @staticmethod
1607    def read_data(
1608            filename: str,
1609            data_representation: DataRepresentation
1610        ) -> Union['spacepy.datamodel', 'xr.Dataset']:
1611        """
1612        Reads the data from the given file.
1613
1614        Parameters
1615        ----------
1616        filename
1617            Name of file to read.
1618        data_representation
1619            Requested data representation.
1620        Returns
1621        -------
1622        spacepy.datamodel or xr.Dataset
1623            Data from file.
1624        Raises
1625        ------
1626        Exception
1627            If an Exception is raise by either the spdm.fromCDF() or
1628            cdflib.cdf_to_xarray() functions.
1629        ModuleNotFoundError
1630            If the required spacepy.datamodel or the cdflib and xarray
1631            modules are not installed.
1632        """
1633        if data_representation is None:
1634            if SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1635                return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1636            if CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1637                return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1638                                     fillval_to_nan=True)
1639            raise ModuleNotFoundError(
1640                'neither the spacepy.datamodel nor the cdflib and '
1641                'xarray modules are installed')
1642
1643        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY and \
1644           not SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1645            raise ModuleNotFoundError('spacepy module must be installed')
1646        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY and \
1647           not CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1648            raise ModuleNotFoundError('cdflib and xarray modules must be installed')
1649
1650        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY:
1651            return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1652        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY:
1653            return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1654                                 fillval_to_nan=True)
1655        return None
1656
1657
1658    def get_data(
1659            self,
1660            dataset: str,
1661            variables: List[str],
1662            time0: Union[TimeInterval, List[TimeInterval], datetime, str],
1663            time1: Union[datetime, str] = None,
1664            **keywords: Union[
1665                Dict,
1666                DataRepresentation,
1667                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1668                Any]
1669        ) -> Tuple[Dict, 'spdm.SpaceData', 'xarray']:
1670        """
1671        Gets the specified data from the server.  The representation
1672        of the returned data is determined as follows:<br>
1673        1. If a dataRepresentation keyword parameter is given, its
1674           value will determine the representation of the returned
1675           data.  If no dataRepresenation keyword parameter is
1676           given, then<br>
1677        2. If the presence of spacepy.datamodel is found, then the data
1678           is returned in the spacepy.datamodel representation.<br>
1679        3. If the presence of the cdflib and xarray modules are found,
1680           then the data is returned in an xarray.Dataset.
1681
1682        Parameters
1683        ----------
1684        dataset
1685            dataset identifier of data to get.
1686        variables
1687            array containing names of variables to get.  The value
1688            ALL-VARIABLES may be used instead of specifying all the
1689            individual variable names.
1690        time0
1691            TimeInterval(s) or start time of data to get.  See module
1692            note about timezone.
1693        time1
1694            when time0 is not one or more TimeInterval(s), the end time
1695            of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1696        keywords
1697            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1698            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1699            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1700            the given binning parameter values.  See
1701            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/CDAWeb_Binning_readme.html>
1702            for more details.  binData is a Dict that
1703            may contain the following keys: interval,
1704            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1705            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1706            defaults.<br>
1707            <b>dataRepresentation</b> - specifies the representation of
1708            the returned data as one of
1709            `datarepresentation.DataRepresentation`.<br>
1710            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1711            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1712            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1713            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1714            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1715            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1716            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1717            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1718            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1719            the progress of this call.<br>
1720            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1721            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1722        Returns
1723        -------
1724        Tuple
1725            [0] contains a dictionary of HTTP and CDAS status information.
1726            When successful, ['http']['status_code'] will be 200.<br>
1727            [1] contains the requested data (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset
1728            object) or None.
1729        Raises
1730        ------
1731        ValueError
1732            If no variables are given or if the given start/end datetime
1733            values are invalid.
1734        """
1735        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1736        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1737        # pylint: disable=too-many-statements
1738        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1739        # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
1740
1741        #import spacepy.datamodel as spdm       # type: ignore
1742
1743        if len(variables) < 1:
1744            raise ValueError('at least one variable name is required')
1745
1746        if isinstance(time0, (str, datetime)):
1747            if isinstance(time1, (str, datetime)):
1748                time_intervals = [TimeInterval(time0, time1)]
1749            else:
1750                raise ValueError('time1 must be str/datetime')
1751        elif isinstance(time0, TimeInterval):
1752            time_intervals = [time0]
1753        elif isinstance(time0, list) and len(time0) > 0 and\
1754             isinstance(time0[0], TimeInterval):
1755            time_intervals = time0
1756        else:
1757            raise ValueError('invalid time0 type')
1758
1759        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1760                                  time_intervals,
1761                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1762                                  binData=keywords.get('binData', {}))
1763
1764        data_rep = keywords.get('dataRepresentation', None)
1765        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1766        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1767
1768        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1769
1770        status = {
1771            'http': {
1772                'status_code': 204
1773            },
1774            'cdas': {
1775                'status': [],
1776                'message': [],
1777                'warning': [],
1778                'error': []
1779            }
1780        }
1781
1782        if progress_callback is not None:
1783            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making initial server request.',
1784                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1785                return (status, None)
1786
1787        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1788                                                        progress_callback,
1789                                                        progress_user_value)
1790
1791        status['http']['status_code'] = status_code
1792
1793        if progress_callback is not None:
1794            if progress_callback(0.3, 'Initial server request complete.',
1795                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1796                return (status, None)
1797
1798        if status_code != 200:
1799
1800            self.logger.info('get_data_result failed with http code %d',
1801                             status_code)
1802            self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1803            return (status, None)
1804
1805        if not data_result:
1806            return (status, None)
1807
1808        if 'Status' in data_result:
1809            status['cdas']['status'] = data_result['Status']
1810        if 'Message' in data_result:
1811            status['cdas']['message'] = data_result['Message']
1812        if 'Warning' in data_result:
1813            status['cdas']['warning'] = data_result['Warning']
1814        if 'Error' in data_result:
1815            status['cdas']['error'] = data_result['Error']
1816
1817        if progress_callback is not None:
1818            if progress_callback(0.4, 'Beginning download of data.',
1819                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1820                return (status, None)
1821
1822        file_descriptions = data_result['FileDescription']
1823
1824        data_url = file_descriptions[0]['Name']
1825        data_length = file_descriptions[0]['Length']
1826
1827        self.logger.debug('data_url = %s, data_length = %d',
1828                          data_url, data_length)
1829
1830        sub_progress_control = {
1831            'progressCallback': progress_callback,
1832            'progressUserValue': progress_user_value,
1833            'progressStart': 0.4,
1834            'progressFraction': 0.1
1835        }
1836
1837        tmp_filename = self.download(data_url, data_length,
1838                                     progressCallback=_get_data_progress,
1839                                     progressUserValue=sub_progress_control)
1840
1841        try:
1842            data = self.read_data(tmp_filename, data_rep)
1843            os.remove(tmp_filename)
1844            if progress_callback is not None:
1845                if progress_callback(1.0, 'Finished reading data.',
1846                                     progress_user_value) != 0:
1847                    return (status, None)
1848        except:
1849            self.logger.error('Exception from read_data(%s): %s, %s',
1850                              tmp_filename, sys.exc_info()[0],
1851                              sys.exc_info()[1])
1852            self.logger.error('CDF file has been retained.')
1853            raise
1854        return (status, data)
1855
1856
1857    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
1858    def get_graph(
1859            self,
1860            dataset: str,
1861            variables: List[str],
1862            start: Union[datetime, str],
1863            end: Union[datetime, str],
1864            options: GraphOptions = None,
1865            image_format: List[ImageFormat] = None,
1866            **keywords
1867        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1868        """
1869        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1870        server.
1871
1872        Parameters
1873        ----------
1874        dataset
1875            dataset identifier of data to get.
1876        variables
1877            array containing names of variables to get.
1878        start
1879            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1880        end
1881            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1882        options
1883            graph options.
1884        image_format
1885            image format.  If None, then [ImageFormat.PNG].
1886        keywords
1887            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1888            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1889            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1890            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1891            may contain the following keys: interval,
1892            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1893            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1894            defaults.<br>
1895            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1896            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1897            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1898            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1899            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1900            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1901            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1902            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1903            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1904            the progress of this call.<br>
1905            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
1906            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1907        Returns
1908        -------
