1909        Tuple
1910            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
1911            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
1912                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1913                DataResult object or None.<br>
1914        Raises
1915        ------
1916        ValueError
1917            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1918        """
1919        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1920        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1921        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1922        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1923
1924        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1925                                                                  end)
1926
1927        request = GraphRequest(dataset, variables,
1928                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
1929                               options, image_format,
1930                               **keywords)
1931
1932        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1933        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1934
1935        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
1936
1937        if progress_callback is not None:
1938            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1939                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1940                return (204, None)
1941
1942        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request, progress_callback, progress_user_value)
1943
1944        if progress_callback is not None:
1945            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
1946                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1947                return (status_code, None)
1948
1949        if status_code != 200:
1950
1951            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
1952                             status_code)
1953            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
1954            return (status_code, None)
1955
1956        return (status_code, result)
1957    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
1958
1959
1960    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
1961    def get_thumbnail(
1962            self,
1963            dataset: str,
1964            variables: List[str],
1965            start: Union[datetime, str],
1966            end: Union[datetime, str],
1967            identifier: str,
1968            thumbnail: int = 1,
1969            **keywords
1970        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1971        """
1972        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1973        server.
1974
1975        Parameters
1976        ----------
1977        dataset
1978            dataset identifier of data to get.
1979        variables
1980            array containing names of variables to get.
1981        start
1982            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1983        end
1984            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1985        identifier
1986            thumbnail identifier (returned in a previous get_graph
1987            result).
1988        thumbnail
1989            number of thumbnail whose full size image is being requested.
1990            Thumbnail images are counted beginning at one (not zero).
1991        keywords
1992            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1993            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1994            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1995            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1996            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1997            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1998            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1999            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2000            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2001            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2002            the progress of this call.<br>
2003            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2004            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2005        Returns
2006        -------
2007        Tuple
2008            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2009            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2010                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2011                DataResult object or None.<br>
2012        Raises
2013        ------
2014        ValueError
2015            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2016        """
2017        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2018        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2019        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2020        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2021
2022        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2023                                                                  end)
2024
2025        request = ThumbnailRequest(dataset, variables,
2026                                   TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2027                                   identifier, thumbnail)
2028
2029        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2030        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2031
2032        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2033
2034        if progress_callback is not None:
2035            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2036                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2037                return (204, None)
2038
2039        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2040                                                   progress_callback,
2041                                                   progress_user_value)
2042
2043        if progress_callback is not None:
2044            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2045                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2046                return (status_code, None)
2047
2048        if status_code != 200:
2049
2050            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2051                             status_code)
2052            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2053            return (status_code, None)
2054
2055        return (status_code, result)
2056    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
2057
2058
2059    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
2060    def get_text(
2061            self,
2062            dataset: str,
2063            variables: List[str],
2064            start: Union[datetime, str],
2065            end: Union[datetime, str],
2066            compression: Compression = Compression.UNCOMPRESSED,
2067            text_format: TextFormat = TextFormat.PLAIN,
2068            **keywords
2069        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2070        """
2071        Gets a textual representation of the specified data from the
2072        server.
2073
2074        Parameters
2075        ----------
2076        dataset
2077            dataset identifier of data to get.
2078        variables
2079            array containing names of variables to get.
2080        start
2081            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2082        end
2083            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2084        compression
2085            file compression.
2086        text_format
2087            text format.
2088        keywords
2089            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2090            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2091            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2092            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2093            may contain the following keys: interval,
2094            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2095            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2096            defaults.<br>
2097            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2098            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2099            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2100            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2101            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2102            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2103            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2104            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2105            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2106            the progress of this call.<br>
2107            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2108            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2109        Returns
2110        -------
2111        Tuple
2112            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2113            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2114                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2115                DataResult object or None.<br>
2116        Raises
2117        ------
2118        ValueError
2119            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2120        """
2121        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2122        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2123        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2124        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2125
2126        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2127                                                                  end)
2128
2129        request = TextRequest(dataset, variables,
2130                              TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2131                              compression, text_format,
2132                              **keywords)
2133
2134        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2135        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2136
2137        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2138
2139        if progress_callback is not None:
2140            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2141                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2142                return (204, None)
2143
2144        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2145                                                   progress_callback,
2146                                                   progress_user_value)
2147
2148        if progress_callback is not None:
2149            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2150                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2151                return (status_code, None)
2152
2153        if status_code != 200:
2154
2155            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2156                             status_code)
2157            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2158            return (status_code, None)
2159
2160        return (status_code, result)
2161    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
2162
2163
2164    def get_audio(
2165            self,
2166            dataset: str,
2167            variables: List[str],
2168            start: Union[datetime, str],
2169            end: Union[datetime, str],
2170            **keywords
2171        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2172        """
2173        Gets an audio representation of the specified data from the
2174        server.
2175
2176        Parameters
2177        ----------
2178        dataset
2179            dataset identifier of data to get.
2180        variables
2181            array containing names of variables to get.
2182        start
2183            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2184        end
2185            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2186        keywords
2187            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2188            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2189            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2190            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2191            may contain the following keys: interval,
2192            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2193            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2194            defaults.<br>
2195            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2196            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2197            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2198            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2199            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2200            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2201            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2202            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2203            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2204            the progress of this call.<br>
2205            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2206            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2207        Returns
2208        -------
2209        Tuple
2210            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2211            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2212                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2213                DataResult object or None.<br>
2214        Raises
2215        ------
2216        ValueError
2217            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2218        """
2219        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2220        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2221        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2222        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2223
2224        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2225                                                                  end)
2226
2227        request = AudioRequest(dataset, variables,
2228                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2229                               **keywords)
2230
2231        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2232        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2233
2234        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2235
2236        if progress_callback is not None:
2237            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2238                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2239                return (204, None)
2240
2241        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2242                                                   progress_callback,
2243                                                   progress_user_value)
2244
2245        if progress_callback is not None:
2246            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2247                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2248                return (status_code, None)
2249
2250        if status_code != 200:
2251
2252            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2253                             status_code)
2254            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2255            return (status_code, None)
2256
2257        return (status_code, result)
2258
2259
2260    def get_original_files(
2261            self,
2262            dataset: str,
2263            start: Union[datetime, str],
2264            end: Union[datetime, str],
2265            **keywords
2266        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2267        """
2268        Gets original data files from a dataset.  Original data files
2269        lack updated meta-data and virtual variable values contained
2270        in files obtained from the `CdasWs.get_data`.  Most callers
2271        should probably use `CdasWs.get_data` instead of this function.
2272
2273        Parameters
2274        ----------
2275        dataset
2276            dataset identifier of data to get.
2277        start
2278            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2279        end
2280            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2281        keywords
2282            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2283            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2284            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2285            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2286            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2287            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2288            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2289            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2290            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2291            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2292            the progress of this call.<br>
2293            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2294            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2295        Returns
2296        -------
2297        Tuple
2298            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2299            [1] array of dictionary representations of a
2300                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2301                FileDescription objects or None.<br>
2302        Raises
2303        ------
2304        ValueError
2305            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2306        See Also
2307        --------
2308        CdasWs.get_data
2309        """
2310        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2311        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2312        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2313        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2314
2315        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2316                                                                  end)
2317
2318        request = CdfRequest(dataset, [],
2319                             TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime))
2320
2321        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2322        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2323
2324        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2325
2326        if progress_callback is not None:
2327            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2328                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2329                return (204, None)
2330
2331        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2332                                                   progress_callback,
2333                                                   progress_user_value)
2334
2335        if progress_callback is not None:
2336            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2337                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2338                return (status_code, None)
2339
2340        if status_code != 200:
2341
2342            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2343                             status_code)
2344            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2345            return (status_code, None)
2346
2347        return (status_code, result['FileDescription'])
2348
2349
2350    def get_ssc_id(
2351            self,
2352            dataset: str
2353        ) -> Tuple[int, Union[str, List[str]]]:
2354        """
2355        Gets the Satellite Situation Center (SSC)
2356        <https://sscweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/> observatory identifier(s)
2357        associated with the given cdaweb dataset identifier.
2358
2359        Notes
2360        -----
2361        This method relies upon the Heliophysics Data Portal's
2362        <https://heliophysicsdata.gsfc.nasa.gov/> metadata.  That metadata
2363        may be incomplete.  Also, cdaweb has datasets for which SSC has
2364        no corresponding observatory (for example, ground observatory
2365        data).  Callers should be prepared for negative results (200, None)
2366        from this method.
2367
2368        Parameters
2369        ----------
2370        dataset
2371            cdaweb dataset identifier.
2372        Returns
2373        -------
2374        Tuple
2375            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2376            [1] the SSC observatory identifier(s) associated with the given
2377                cdaweb dataset identifier or None if none is found.
2378        """
2379        url = self._hdp_registry + '?cdawebId=' + dataset
2380
2381        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
2382
2383        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
2384
2385        if response.status_code != 200:
2386
2387            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
2388                             response.status_code)
2389            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
2390            return (response.status_code, None)
2391
2392        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
2393            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
2394
2395        results = ET.fromstring(response.text)
2396
2397        ssc_id = []
2398        for ssc_id_elem in results.findall('SscId'):
2399            ssc_id.append(ssc_id_elem.text)
2400
2401        if len(ssc_id) == 0:
2402            result = None
2403        elif len(ssc_id) == 1:
2404            result = ssc_id[0]
2405        else:
2406            result = ssc_id
2407
2408        return (response.status_code, result)
class NullAuth(requests.auth.AuthBase): 
186class NullAuth(requests.auth.AuthBase): # pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods
187    """
188    Authentication class used to cause requests to ignore any ~/.netrc
189    file.  The CDAS web services do not support authentication and
190    a cdaweb (ftps) entry will cause CdasWs requests to fail with
191    a 401 error.  See <https://github.com/psf/requests/issues/2773>.
192    """
193    def __call__(self, r):
194        return r

Authentication class used to cause requests to ignore any ~/.netrc file. The CDAS web services do not support authentication and a cdaweb (ftps) entry will cause CdasWs requests to fail with a 401 error. See https://github.com/psf/requests/issues/2773.

class CdasWs: 
 197class CdasWs:
 198    """
 199    Class representing the web service interface to NASA's
 200    Coordinated Data Analysis System (CDAS)
 201    <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov>.
 202
 203    Notes
 204    -----
 205    The logger used by this class has the class' name (CdasWs).  By default,
 206    it is configured with a NullHandler.  Users of this class may configure
 207    the logger to aid in diagnosing problems.
 208    """
 209    # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes
 210    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
 211    def __init__(
 212            self,
 213            endpoint=None,
 214            timeout=None,
 215            proxy=None,
 216            ca_certs=None,
 217            disable_ssl_certificate_validation=False,
 218            user_agent=None,
 219            disable_cache=False):
 220        """
 221        Creates an object representing the CDAS web services.
 222
 223        Parameters
 224        ----------
 225        endpoint
 226            URL of the CDAS web service.  If None, the default is
 227            'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'.
 228        timeout
 229            Number of seconds to wait for a response from the server.
 230        proxy
 231            HTTP proxy information.  For example,<pre>
 232            proxies = {
 233              'http': 'http://10.10.1.10:3128',
 234              'https': 'http://10.10.1.10:1080',
 235            }</pre>
 236            Proxy information can also be set with environment variables.
 237            For example,<pre>
 238            $ export HTTP_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:3128"
 239            $ export HTTPS_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:1080"</pre>
 240        ca_certs
 241            Path to certificate authority (CA) certificates that will
 242            override the default bundle.
 243        disable_ssl_certificate_validation
 244            Flag indicating whether to validate the SSL certificate.
 245        user_agent
 246            A value that is appended to the HTTP User-Agent value.
 247        disable_cache
 248            Flag indicating whether to disable HTTP caching.
 249        """
 250
 251        self.logger = logging.getLogger(type(self).__name__)
 252        self.logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
 253
 254        self.logger.debug('endpoint = %s', endpoint)
 255        self.logger.debug('ca_certs = %s', ca_certs)
 256        self.logger.debug('disable_ssl_certificate_validation = %s',
 257                          disable_ssl_certificate_validation)
 258        self.logger.debug('disable_cache = %s', disable_cache)
 259
 260        if endpoint is None:
 261            self._endpoint = 'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'
 262        else:
 263            self._endpoint = endpoint
 264
 265        self._user_agent = 'cdasws/' + __version__ + ' (' + \
 266            platform.python_implementation() + ' ' \
 267            + platform.python_version() + '; ' + platform.platform() + ')'
 268
 269        if user_agent is not None:
 270            self._user_agent += ' (' + user_agent + ')'
 271
 272        self._request_headers = {
 273            #'Content-Type' : 'application/json',
 274            'Content-Type' : 'application/xml',
 275            'Accept' : 'application/xml',
 276            'User-Agent' : self._user_agent,
 277            #'Accept-Encoding' : 'gzip'  # only beneficial for icdfml responses
 278        }
 279        if CACHE_AVAILABLE and disable_cache is not True:
 280            self._session = requests_cache.CachedSession('cdasws_cache',
 281                                                         cache_control=True)
 282        else:
 283            self._session = requests.Session()
 284
 285        self._session.headers.update(self._request_headers)
 286        self._session.auth = NullAuth()
 287
 288        if ca_certs is not None:
 289            self._session.verify = ca_certs
 290
 291        if disable_ssl_certificate_validation is True:
 292            self._session.verify = False
 293
 294        if proxy is not None:
 295            self._proxy = proxy
 296
 297        self._timeout = timeout
 298
 299        endpoint_components = urlparse(self._endpoint)
 300        self._hdp_registry = endpoint_components.scheme + '://' + \
 301            endpoint_components.netloc + '/registry/hdp/SscId.xql'
 302
 303    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
 304
 305
 306    def __del__(self):
 307        """
 308        Destructor.  Closes all network connections.
 309        """
 310
 311        self.close()
 312
 313
 314    def close(self) -> None:
 315        """
 316        Closes any persistent network connections.  Generally, deleting
 317        this object is sufficient and calling this method is unnecessary.
 318        """
 319        self._session.close()
 320
 321
 322    def get_observatory_groups(
 323            self,
 324            **keywords: str
 325        ) -> List[Dict]:
 326        """
 327        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups from the server.
 328
 329        Parameters
 330        ----------
 331        keywords
 332            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 333            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 334            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting
 335            this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 336            based upon their instrumentType value.
 337        Returns
 338        -------
 339        List
 340            An array of ObservatoryGroupDescription
 341            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 342            ObservatoryGroupDescription in
 343            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 344        """
 345        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 346            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups?instrumentType=' + \
 347                      urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 348        else:
 349            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups'
 350
 351        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 352
 353        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 354
 355        if response.status_code != 200:
 356
 357            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 358                             response.status_code)
 359            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 360            return []
 361
 362        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 363            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 364
 365        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 366
 367        observatory_group_descriptions = []
 368        for description in observatory_response.findall(\
 369            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupDescription', namespaces=NS):
 370
 371            observatory_ids = []
 372            for observatory_id in description.findall(\
 373                'cdas:ObservatoryId', namespaces=NS):
 374
 375                observatory_ids.append(observatory_id.text)
 376
 377            observatory_group_descriptions.append({
 378                'Name': description.find(\
 379                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 380                'ObservatoryId': observatory_ids
 381            })
 382
 383        return observatory_group_descriptions
 384
 385
 386    def get_instrument_types(
 387            self,
 388            **keywords: str
 389        ) -> List[Dict]:
 390        """
 391        Gets descriptions of the instrument types from the server.
 392
 393        Parameters
 394        ----------
 395        keywords
 396            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 397            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
 398            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
 399            indicates that no instrumentTypes are eliminated based upon
 400            their observatory value.<br>
 401            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from
 402            those returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting
 403            this parameter indicates that no instrumentTypes are
 404            eliminated based upon their observatoryGroup value.</br>
 405        Returns
 406        -------
 407        List
 408            An array of InstrumentTypeDescription
 409            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 410            InstrumentTypeDescription in
 411            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 412        """
 413        if 'observatory' in keywords:
 414            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatory=' \
 415                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
 416        elif 'observatoryGroup' in keywords:
 417            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatoryGroup=' \
 418                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatoryGroup'])
 419        else:
 420            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes'
 421
 422        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 423
 424        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 425
 426        if response.status_code != 200:
 427
 428            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 429                             response.status_code)
 430            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 431            return []
 432
 433        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 434            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 435
 436        instrument_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 437
 438        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 439            self.logger.debug('instrument_response = %s',
 440                              ET.tostring(instrument_response))
 441
 442        instrument_types = []
 443        for description in instrument_response.findall(\
 444            'cdas:InstrumentTypeDescription', namespaces=NS):
 445
 446            instrument_types.append({
 447                'Name': description.find('cdas:Name',
 448                                         namespaces=NS).text
 449                })
 450        return instrument_types
 451
 452
 453    def get_instruments(
 454            self,
 455            **keywords: str
 456        ) -> List[Dict]:
 457        """
 458        Gets descriptions of the instruments from the server.
 459
 460        Parameters
 461        ----------
 462        keywords
 463            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 464            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
 465            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
 466            indicates that no instruments are eliminated based upon their
 467            observatory value.<br>
 468            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 469            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 470            parameter indicates that no instruments are eliminated based
 471            upon their instrument type.<br>
 472        Returns
 473        -------
 474        List
 475            An array of InstrumentDescription
 476            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 477            InstrumentDescription in
 478            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 479        """
 480        if 'observatory' in keywords:
 481            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?observatory=' \
 482                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
 483        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 484            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?instrumentType=' \
 485                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 486        else:
 487            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments'
 488
 489        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 490
 491        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 492
 493        if response.status_code != 200:
 494
 495            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 496                             response.status_code)
 497            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 498            return []
 499
 500        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 501            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 502
 503        instruments_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 504
 505        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 506            self.logger.debug('instruments = %s', response.text)
 507                              #ET.indent(instruments_response, space='    '))
 508
 509        instruments = []
 510        for instrument_description in instruments_response.findall(\
 511            'cdas:InstrumentDescription', namespaces=NS):
 512
 513            instruments.append({
 514                'Name': instrument_description.find(\
 515                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 516                'ShortDescription': instrument_description.find(\
 517                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
 518                'LongDescription': instrument_description.find(\
 519                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
 520            })
 521
 522        return instruments
 523
 524
 525    def get_observatories(
 526            self,
 527            **keywords: str
 528        ) -> List[Dict]:
 529        """
 530        Gets descriptions of the observatories from the server.
 531
 532        Parameters
 533        ----------
 534        keywords
 535            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 536            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned
 537            by `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter
 538            indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon
 539            their instrument value.<br>
 540            <b>instrumentType</b> - in instrument type value from those
 541            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 542            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 543            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 544        Returns
 545        -------
 546        List
 547            An array of ObservatoryDescriptions
 548            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 549            ObservatoryDescription in
 550            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 551        """
 552        if 'instrument' in keywords:
 553            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrument=' \
 554                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrument'])
 555        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 556            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrumentType=' \
 557                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 558        else:
 559            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories'
 560
 561        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 562
 563        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 564
 565        if response.status_code != 200:
 566
 567            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 568                             response.status_code)
 569            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 570            return []
 571
 572        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 573            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 574
 575        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 576
 577        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 578            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
 579
 580        observatories = []
 581
 582        for observatory in observatory_response.findall(\
 583            'cdas:ObservatoryDescription', namespaces=NS):
 584            observatories.append({
 585                'Name': observatory.find(\
 586                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
 587                'ShortDescription': observatory.find(\
 588                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
 589                'LongDescription': observatory.find(\
 590                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
 591            })
 592
 593        return observatories
 594
 595
 596    def get_observatory_groups_and_instruments(
 597            self,
 598            **keywords: str
 599        ) -> List[Dict]:
 600        """
 601        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups (and associated
 602        instruments) from the server.
 603
 604        Parameters
 605        ----------
 606        keywords
 607            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 608            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 609            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 610            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
 611            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 612        Returns
 613        -------
 614        List
 615            An array of ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription
 616            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
 617            ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription in
 618            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 619        """
 620        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
 621            url = self._endpoint \
 622                  + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments?instrumentType=' \
 623                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
 624        else:
 625            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments'
 626
 627        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
 628
 629        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 630
 631        if response.status_code != 200:
 632
 633            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 634                             response.status_code)
 635            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 636            return []
 637
 638        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 639            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 640
 641        observatories_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 642
 643        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 644            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
 645
 646        o_g_i_ds = []
 647
 648        for o_g_i_d in observatories_response.findall(\
 649            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription',\
 650            namespaces=NS):
 651
 652            o_g_i_d_name = o_g_i_d.find('cdas:Name',
 653                                        namespaces=NS).text
 654            o_is = []
 655            for o_i in o_g_i_d.findall('cdas:ObservatoryInstruments',
 656                                       namespaces=NS):
 657
 658                o_i_name = o_i.find('cdas:Name',
 659                                    namespaces=NS).text
 660                i_ds = []
 661                for i_d in o_i.findall('cdas:InstrumentDescription',
 662                                       namespaces=NS):
 663                    i_d_name = i_d.find('cdas:Name',
 664                                        namespaces=NS).text
 665                    i_d_short_description = \
 666                        i_d.find('cdas:ShortDescription',
 667                                 namespaces=NS).text
 668                    i_d_long_description = \
 669                         i_d.find('cdas:LongDescription',
 670                                  namespaces=NS).text
 671                    i_ds.append({
 672                        'Name': i_d_name,
 673                        'ShortDescription': i_d_short_description,
 674                        'LongDescription': i_d_long_description
 675                    })
 676                o_is.append({
 677                    'Name': o_i_name,
 678                    'InstrumentDescription': i_ds
 679                })
 680
 681            o_g_i_ds.append({
 682                'Name': o_g_i_d_name,
 683                'ObservatoryInstruments': o_is
 684            })
 685
 686        return o_g_i_ds
 687
 688
 689    # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
 690    def get_datasets(
 691            self,
 692            **keywords: str
 693        ) -> List[Dict]:
 694        """
 695        Gets descriptions of the specified datasets from the server.
 696
 697        Parameters
 698        ----------
 699        keywords
 700            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
 701            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from those
 702            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting this
 703            parameter
 704            indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their
 705            observatoryGroup value.<br>
 706            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
 707            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
 708            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
 709            upon their instrumentType value.<br>
 710            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory name value from those
 711            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this
 712            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
 713            upon their observatory value.<br>
 714            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned by
 715            `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter indicates
 716            that no datasets are eliminated based upon their instrument
 717            value.<br>
 718            <b>startDate</b> - a datetime specifying the start of a time
 719            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
 720            parameter is ommited, the time interval will begin infinitely
 721            in the past.<br>
 722            <b>stopDate</b> - a datetime specifying the end of a time
 723            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
 724            parameter is omitted, the time interval will end infinitely
 725            in the future.<br>
 726            <b>id</b> - a dataset identifier. The value may be a CDAS
 727            (e.g., AC_H2_MFI), DOI (e.g., 10.48322/fh85-fj47), or SPASE
 728            [ResourceID] (e.g., spase://NASA/NumericalData/ACE/MAG/L2/PT1H)
 729            identifier. If specified, all other keywords are ignored.<br>
 730            <b>idPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 731            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS identifier value.
 732            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.<br>
 733            <b>labelPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 734            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS label text.
 735            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
 736            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?i)` for case insensitive
 737            match mode) are supported and likely to be useful in this
 738            case.<br>
 739            <b>notesPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
 740            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS notes text.
 741            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
 742            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?s)` for dotall match mode)
 743            are supported and likely to be useful in this case.<br>
 744        Returns
 745        -------
 746        List
 747            A list of dictionaries containing descriptions of the datasets
 748            requested.  The dictionary structure is defined by the
 749            DatasetDescription element in
 750            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
 751        """
 752        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets?'
 753
 754        observatory_groups = keywords.get('observatoryGroup', None)
 755        if observatory_groups is not None:
 756            if isinstance(observatory_groups, str):
 757                observatory_groups = [observatory_groups]
 758            for observatory_group in observatory_groups:
 759                url = url + 'observatoryGroup=' \
 760                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory_group) + '&'
 761
 762        instrument_types = keywords.get('instrumentType', None)
 763        if instrument_types is not None:
 764            if isinstance(instrument_types, str):
 765                instrument_types = [instrument_types]
 766            for instrument_type in instrument_types:
 767                url = url + 'instrumentType=' \
 768                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument_type) + '&'
 769
 770        observatories = keywords.get('observatory', None)
 771        if observatories is not None:
 772            if isinstance(observatories, str):
 773                observatories = [observatories]
 774            for observatory in observatories:
 775                url = url + 'observatory=' \
 776                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory) + '&'
 777
 778        instruments = keywords.get('instrument', None)
 779        if instruments is not None:
 780            if isinstance(instruments, str):
 781                instruments = [instruments]
 782            for instrument in instruments:
 783                url = url + 'instrument=' \
 784                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument) + '&'
 785
 786        if 'startDate' in keywords:
 787            url = url + 'startDate=' \
 788                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['startDate']) + '&'
 789
 790        if 'stopDate' in keywords:
 791            url = url + 'stopDate=' \
 792                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['stopDate']) + '&'
 793
 794        if 'id' in keywords:
 795            url = url + 'id=' \
 796                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['id']) + '&'
 797
 798        if 'idPattern' in keywords:
 799            url = url + 'idPattern=' \
 800                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['idPattern']) + '&'
 801
 802        if 'labelPattern' in keywords:
 803            url = url + 'labelPattern=' \
 804                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['labelPattern']) + '&'
 805
 806        if 'notesPattern' in keywords:
 807            url = url + 'notesPattern=' \
 808                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['notesPattern']) + '&'
 809
 810        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url[:-1])
 811
 812        response = self._session.get(url[:-1], timeout=self._timeout)
 813
 814        if response.status_code != 200:
 815
 816            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
 817                             response.status_code)
 818            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
 819            return []
 820
 821        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 822            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
 823
 824        dss = ET.fromstring(response.text)
 825
 826        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
 827            self.logger.debug('datasets = %s', response.text)
 828
 829        datasets = []
 830        for ds in dss.findall('cdas:DatasetDescription',
 831                              namespaces=NS):
 832
 833            observatory_groups = []
 834            for o_g in ds.findall('cdas:ObservatoryGroup',
 835                                  namespaces=NS):
 836                observatory_groups.append(o_g.text)
 837
 838            instrument_types = []
 839            for i_t in ds.findall('cdas:InstrumentType',
 840                                  namespaces=NS):
 841                instrument_types.append(i_t.text)
 842
 843            dataset_links = []
 844            for d_l in ds.findall('cdas:DatasetLink',
 845                                  namespaces=NS):
 846                dataset_links.append({
 847                    'Title': d_l.find('cdas:Title',
 848                                      namespaces=NS).text,
 849                    'Text': d_l.find('cdas:Text',
 850                                     namespaces=NS).text,
 851                    'Url': d_l.find('cdas:Url',
 852                                    namespaces=NS).text,
 853                })
 854
 855            observatories = []
 856            for obs_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Observatory',
 857                                       namespaces=NS):
 858                observatories.append(obs_elem.text)
 859
 860            instruments = []
 861            for instr_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Instrument',
 862                                         namespaces=NS):
 863                instruments.append(instr_elem.text)
 864
 865            dataset = {
 866                'Id': ds.find('cdas:Id', namespaces=NS).text,
 867                'Observatory': observatories,
 868                'Instrument': instruments,
 869                'ObservatoryGroup': observatory_groups,
 870                'InstrumentType': instrument_types,
 871                'Label': ds.find('cdas:Label',
 872                                 namespaces=NS).text,
 873                'TimeInterval': {
 874                    'Start': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:Start',
 875                                     namespaces=NS).text,
 876                    'End': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:End',
 877                                   namespaces=NS).text
 878                },
 879                'PiName': ds.find('cdas:PiName',
 880                                  namespaces=NS).text,
 881                'PiAffiliation': ds.find('cdas:PiAffiliation',
 882                                         namespaces=NS).text,
 883                'Notes': ds.find('cdas:Notes',
 884                                 namespaces=NS).text,
 885                'DatasetLink': dataset_links
 886            }
 887            doi = ds.find('cdas:Doi', namespaces=NS)
 888            if doi is not None:
 889                dataset['Doi'] = doi.text
 890
 891            spase_resource_id = ds.find('cdas:SpaseResourceId',
 892                                        namespaces=NS)
 893            if spase_resource_id is not None:
 894                dataset['SpaseResourceId'] = spase_resource_id.text
 895
 896            additional_metadata = []
 897            for add_meta in ds.findall('cdas:AdditionalMetadata',
 898                                       namespaces=NS):
 899                meta_type = add_meta.attrib['Type']
 900                value = add_meta.text
 901                additional_metadata.append({
 902                    'Type': meta_type,
 903                    'value': value
 904                })
 905
 906            if len(additional_metadata) > 0:
 907                dataset['AdditionalMetadata'] = additional_metadata
 908
 909            datasets.append(dataset)
 910
 911        return sorted(datasets, key=itemgetter('Id'))
 912    # pylint: enable=too-many-branches
 913
 914
 915    def get_client_library_example(
 916            self,
 917            dataset: str,
 918            language: str = 'Python'
 919        ) -> str:
 920        """
 921        Gets client library example code for the given dataset and
 922        programming language.
 923
 924        Parameters
 925        ----------
 926        dataset
 927            The dataset to use in the example code.  It must be an Id
 928            returned by get_datasets.
 929        language
 930            The programming language of the example code.  It must be
 931            either Python or IDL.
 932
 933        Returns
 934        -------
 935        str
 936            Requested client library example code.
 937        """
 938        library = 'cdaswsPy'
 939        if language.lower() == 'idl':
 940            library = 'cdaswsIdl'
 941
 942        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
 943                dataset + '/clientLibraryExample/' + library
 944
 945        # Do not use session headers (Accept: Application/xml)
 946        response = requests.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
 947
 948        if response.status_code != 200:
 949            self.logger.warning('%s failed with status %d', response.url,
 950                                response.status_code)
 951            return None
 952
 953        return response.text
 954
 955
 956    @staticmethod
 957    def get_doi_landing_page_url(
 958            doi: str
 959        ) -> str:
 960        """
 961        Returns a URL to the given Digital Object Identifier's landing
 962        page (metadata for the DOI).
 963
 964        Note: this method is deprecated.  You should call 
 965        doi.get_doi_landing_page_url directly.
 966
 967        Parameters
 968        ----------
 969        doi
 970            digital object identifier.
 971        Returns
 972        -------
 973        str
 974            A URL to the DOI's landing page.
 975        """
 976
 977        return get_doi_landing_page_url(doi)
 978
 979
 980    @staticmethod
 981    def get_citation(
 982            doi: str
 983        ) -> str:
 984        """
 985        Returns the citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 986
 987        Parameters
 988        ----------
 989        doi
 990            digital object identifier.
 991        Returns
 992        -------
 993        str
 994            The citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 995        """
 996
 997        url = 'https://doi.org/' + doi
 998        headers = {'Accept': 'text/x-bibliography; style=apa'}
 999        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers,
1000                                timeout=30)
1001
1002        return response.text
1003
1004
1005    def get_inventory(
1006            self,
1007            identifier: str,
1008            **keywords: str
1009        ) -> List[TimeInterval]:
1010        """
1011        Gets a description of the specified dataset's data inventory.
1012
1013        Parameters
1014        ----------
1015        identifier
1016            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1017        keywords
1018            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1019            <b>timeInterval</b> - `timeinterval.TimeInterval` to restrict
1020            returned inventory.
1021        Returns
1022        -------
1023        List
1024            An array of `timeinterval.TimeInterval`s when data is
1025            available.
1026        """
1027
1028        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1029                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/inventory'
1030
1031        if 'timeInterval' in keywords:
1032            time_interval_keyword = keywords['timeInterval']
1033            url = url + '/' + \
1034                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.start) + \
1035                  ',' + \
1036                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.end)
1037
1038        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
1039
1040        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1041
1042        if response.status_code != 200:
1043
1044            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1045                             response.status_code)
1046            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1047            return []
1048
1049        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1050            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1051
1052        inventory = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1053        intervals = []
1054        for inventory_desc in inventory.findall(\
1055                                  'cdas:InventoryDescription',
1056                                  namespaces=NS):
1057            for time_interval in inventory_desc.findall(\
1058                                     'cdas:TimeInterval',
1059                                     namespaces=NS):
1060                intervals.append(
1061                    TimeInterval(
1062                        time_interval.find('cdas:Start',
1063                                           namespaces=NS).text,
1064                        time_interval.find('cdas:End',
1065                                           namespaces=NS).text
1066                    )
1067                )
1068
1069        return intervals
1070
1071
1072    def get_example_time_interval(
1073            self,
1074            identifier: str,
1075        ) -> TimeInterval:
1076        """
1077        Gets a small example time interval for the specified dataset.  The
1078        interval is near the end of the dataset's data inventory.  The
1079        returned interval is not guaranteed to have non-fill data for any
1080        specific variable.
1081
1082        Parameters
1083        ----------
1084        identifier
1085            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1086        Returns
1087        -------
1088        timeinterval.TimeInterval
1089            An small example time interval that is likely, but not
1090            guaranteed, to have data or None if an interval cannot be
1091            found.
1092        """
1093
1094        time_intervals = self.get_inventory(identifier)
1095        if len(time_intervals) < 1:
1096            return None
1097        example_interval = time_intervals[-1]
1098        if re.search('MMS[1-4]_.+_BRST_.+', identifier):
1099            time_delta = timedelta(seconds=1)
1100        else:
1101            time_delta = timedelta(hours=2)
1102        example_interval.start = example_interval.end - time_delta
1103        return example_interval
1104
1105
1106    def get_variables(
1107            self,
1108            identifier: str
1109        ) -> List[Dict]:
1110        """
1111        Gets a description of the variables in the specified dataset.
1112
1113        Parameters
1114        ----------
1115        identifier
1116            dataset identifier of data to get.
1117        Returns
1118        -------
1119        List
1120            A List of dictionary descriptions of the variables in
1121            the specified dataset.  The dictionary structure is defined by
1122            the VariableDescription element in
1123            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
1124        """
1125
1126        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1127                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/variables'
1128
1129        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1130
1131        if response.status_code != 200:
1132
1133            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1134                             response.status_code)
1135            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1136            return []
1137
1138        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1139            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1140
1141        var_descriptions = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1142
1143        variables = []
1144        for var_description in var_descriptions.findall(\
1145                                   'cdas:VariableDescription',
1146                                   namespaces=NS):
1147            name = var_description.find('cdas:Name',
1148                                        namespaces=NS).text
1149            short_description = var_description.find(\
1150                                        'cdas:ShortDescription',
1151                                        namespaces=NS).text
1152            if short_description is None:
1153                short_description = ''
1154
1155            long_description = var_description.find(\
1156                                        'cdas:LongDescription',
1157                                        namespaces=NS).text
1158            if long_description is None:
1159                long_description = ''
1160
1161            variables.append({
1162                'Name': name,
1163                'ShortDescription': short_description,
1164                'LongDescription': long_description
1165            })
1166
1167        return variables
1168
1169
1170    def get_variable_names(
1171            self,
1172            identifier: str
1173        ) -> List[str]:
1174        """
1175        Gets the names of the variables in the specified dataset.  This
1176        method is like the get_variables method except that it only returns
1177        the variable names and not the other metadata.
1178
1179        Parameters
1180        ----------
1181        identifier
1182            dataset identifier of data to get.
1183        Returns
1184        -------
1185        List
1186            A List of the names of the variables in the specified dataset.
1187        """
1188
1189        variable_names = []
1190        for variable in self.get_variables(identifier):
1191            variable_names.append(variable['Name'])
1192
1193        return variable_names
1194
1195
1196    @staticmethod
1197    def _get_thumbnail_description_dict(
1198            file_description_elem: ET.Element
1199        ) -> Dict:
1200        """
1201        Gets ThumbnailDescription dictionary representation from the
1202        given FileDescription element.
1203
1204        Parameters
1205        ----------
1206        file_description_elem
1207            a FileDescription Element.
1208        Returns
1209        -------
1210        Dict
1211            a Dictionary representation of the ThumbnailDescription
1212            contained in the given FileDescription element.
1213        """
1214        thumbnail_desc = file_description_elem.find(\
1215                             'cdas:ThumbnailDescription',
1216                             namespaces=NS)
1217        if thumbnail_desc is not None:
1218            time_interval = thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:TimeInterval',
1219                                                namespaces=NS)
1220            start = time_interval.find('cdas:Start',
1221                                       namespaces=NS).text
1222            end = time_interval.find('cdas:End',
1223                                     namespaces=NS).text
1224            return {
1225                'Name': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:Name',
1226                                            namespaces=NS).text,
1227                'Dataset': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:Dataset',
1228                                               namespaces=NS).text,
1229                'TimeInterval': {
1230                    'Start': start,
1231                    'End': end
1232                },
1233                'VarName': thumbnail_desc.find('cdas:VarName',
1234                                               namespaces=NS).text,
1235                'Options': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1236                                   'cdas:Options',
1237                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1238                'NumFrames': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1239                                     'cdas:NumFrames',
1240                                     namespaces=NS).text),
1241                'NumRows': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1242                                   'cdas:NumRows',
1243                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1244                'NumCols': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1245                                   'cdas:NumCols',
1246                                   namespaces=NS).text),
1247                'TitleHeight': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1248                                       'cdas:TitleHeight',
1249                                       namespaces=NS).text),
1250                'ThumbnailHeight': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1251                                           'cdas:ThumbnailHeight',
1252                                           namespaces=NS).text),
1253                'ThumbnailWidth': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1254                                           'cdas:ThumbnailWidth',
1255                                           namespaces=NS).text),
1256                'StartRecord': int(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1257                                       'cdas:StartRecord',
1258                                       namespaces=NS).text),
1259                'MyScale': float(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1260                                     'cdas:MyScale',
1261                                     namespaces=NS).text),
1262                'XyStep': float(thumbnail_desc.find(\
1263                                    'cdas:XyStep',
1264                                    namespaces=NS).text)
1265            }
1266        return None
1267
1268
1269    @staticmethod
1270    def _get_data_result_dict(
1271            xml_data_result: str
1272        ) -> Dict:
1273        """
1274        Gets DataResult dictionary representation from the
1275        given XML DataResult element.
1276
1277        Parameters
1278        ----------
1279        xml_data_result
1280            XML representation of a DataResult.
1281        Returns
1282        -------
1283        Dict
1284            a Dictionary representation of the given XML representation
1285            of a DataResult.
1286        """
1287        data_result = ET.fromstring(xml_data_result)
1288        file_descriptions = []
1289        for file_description in data_result.findall(\
1290            'cdas:FileDescription', namespaces=NS):
1291
1292            dict_file_description = {
1293                'Name': file_description.find('cdas:Name',
1294                                              namespaces=NS).text,
1295                'MimeType': file_description.find(\
1296                                'cdas:MimeType',
1297                                namespaces=NS).text,
1298                'StartTime': file_description.find(\
1299                                 'cdas:StartTime',
1300                                 namespaces=NS).text,
1301                'EndTime': file_description.find(\
1302                               'cdas:EndTime',
1303                               namespaces=NS).text,
1304                'Length': int(file_description.find(\
1305                                  'cdas:Length',
1306                                  namespaces=NS).text),
1307                'LastModified': file_description.find(\
1308                                    'cdas:LastModified',
1309                                    namespaces=NS).text
1310            }
1311            thumbnail_dict = CdasWs._get_thumbnail_description_dict(\
1312                                     file_description)
1313            if thumbnail_dict is not None:
1314                dict_file_description['ThumbnailDescription'] = \
1315                    thumbnail_dict
1316
1317            thumbnail_id_elem = file_description.find(\
1318                                    'cdas:ThumbnailId',
1319                                    namespaces=NS)
1320            if thumbnail_id_elem is not None:
1321                dict_file_description['ThumbnailId'] = \
1322                    thumbnail_id_elem.text
1323
1324            file_descriptions.append(dict_file_description)
1325
1326        if len(file_descriptions) > 0:
1327            return {
1328                'FileDescription': file_descriptions
1329            }
1330        return None
1331
1332
1333    def get_data_result(
1334            self,
1335            data_request: DataRequest,
1336            progress_callback: Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1337            progress_user_value: Any
1338        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1339        """
1340        Submits the given request to the server and returns the result.
1341        This is a relatively low-level method and most callers should
1342        probably use a higher-level method such as get_data.
1343
1344        Parameters
1345        ----------
1346        data_request
1347            data request.
1348        progress_callback
1349            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1350            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1351            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1352            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1353            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1354            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1355            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1356            the progress of this call.
1357        progressUserValue
1358            value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
1359        Returns
1360        -------
1361        Tuple
1362            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1363            successful.<br>
1364            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1365            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1366            or None.
1367        See Also
1368        --------
1369        CdasWs.get_data
1370        """
1371
1372        #self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.json())
1373        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.xml_str())
1374
1375        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets'
1376
1377        for retries in range(RETRY_LIMIT):
1378            #response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.json(),
1379            response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.xml_str(),
1380                                          timeout=self._timeout)
1381
1382            if response.status_code == 200:
1383
1384                data_result = CdasWs._get_data_result_dict(response.text)
1385
1386                if not data_result:
1387                    return (response.status_code, None)
1388
1389                return (response.status_code, data_result)
1390
1391            if response.status_code == 429 or \
1392               response.status_code == 503 and \
1393               'Retry-After' in response.headers:
1394
1395                retry_after = response.headers['Retry-After']
1396
1397                self.logger.debug('429/503 status with Retry-After header: %s',
1398                                  retry_after)
1399
1400                if progress_callback is not None:
1401                    if progress_callback(0.2, 'Waiting ' + retry_after + \
1402                                         's before making server request.',
1403                                         progress_user_value) != 0:
1404                        return (204, None)
1405
1406                retry_after = int(retry_after)
1407
1408                self.logger.info('Sleeping %d seconds before making request',
1409                                 retry_after)
1410                time.sleep(retry_after)
1411
1412            else:
1413                self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1414                                 response.status_code)
1415                self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1416                self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1417                return (response.status_code, None)
1418
1419        self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d after %d retries',
1420                         url, response.status_code, retries + 1)
1421        self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1422        self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1423        return (response.status_code, None)
1424
1425
1426    def get_data_file(
1427            self,
1428            dataset: str,
1429            variables: List[str],
1430            start: Union[datetime, str], end: Union[datetime, str],
1431            **keywords: Union[
1432                Dict,
1433                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1434                Any]
1435        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1436        """
1437        Gets the specified data file from the server.
1438
1439        Parameters
1440        ----------
1441        dataset
1442            dataset identifier of data to get.
1443        variables
1444            array containing names of variables to get.
1445        start
1446            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1447        end
1448            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1449        keywords
1450            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1451            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1452            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1453            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1454            may contain the following keys: interval,
1455            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1456            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1457            defaults.<br>
1458            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1459            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1460            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1461            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1462            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1463            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data_file()
1464            function will immediately return (204, None).  The float
1465            parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress
1466            and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1467            the progress of this call.<br>
1468            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1469            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1470        Returns
1471        -------
1472        Tuple
1473            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1474            successful.<br>
1475            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1476            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1477            or None.
1478        Raises
1479        ------
1480        ValueError
1481            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1482        See Also
1483        --------
1484        CdasWs.get_data : In addition to what get_data_file does,
1485            get_data also downloads and reads the data file into memory
1486            (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset object).
1487        """
1488        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1489        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1490        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1491        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1492
1493        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1494                                                                  end)
1495
1496        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1497                                  TimeInterval(start_datetime,
1498                                               end_datetime),
1499                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1500                                  **keywords.get('binData', {}))
1501
1502        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1503        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1504
1505        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1506
1507        if progress_callback is not None:
1508            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1509                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1510                return (204, None)
1511
1512        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1513                                                        progress_callback,
1514                                                        progress_user_value)
1515
1516        if progress_callback is not None:
1517            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Initial server request complete.',
1518                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1519                return (status_code, None)
1520
1521        return (status_code, data_result)
1522
1523
1524    def download(
1525            self,
1526            url: str,
1527            size: int = 0,
1528            **keywords
1529        ) -> str:
1530        """
1531        Downloads the file specified by the given URL to a temporary
1532        file without reading all of it into memory.  This method
1533        utilizes the connection pool and persistent HTTP connection
1534        to the CdasWs server.
1535
1536        Parameters
1537        ----------
1538        url
1539            URL of file to download.
1540        size
1541            number of bytes in file to download.
1542        keywords
1543            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1544            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1545            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1546            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1547            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1548            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1549            getting the data will be aborted and this download() function
1550            will immediately return None.  The float parameter
1551            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1552            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1553            the progress of this call.<br>
1554            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
