1555            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1556        Returns
1557        -------
1558        str
1559            name of tempory file or None if there was an error.
1560        """
1561        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1562
1563        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1564        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1565
1566        suffix = os.path.splitext(urlparse(url).path)[1]
1567
1568        file_descriptor, tmp_filename = mkstemp(suffix=suffix)
1569
1570        download_bytes = 0
1571        next_progress_report = 0.1
1572        with self._session.get(url, stream=True,
1573                               timeout=self._timeout) as response:
1574
1575            file = open(tmp_filename, 'wb')
1576            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
1577                if chunk:  # filter out keep-alive new chunks
1578                    file.write(chunk)
1579                    # file.flush()
1580                    if progress_callback is not None:
1581                        download_bytes += len(chunk)
1582                        if size == 0:
1583                            download_progress = 0.0
1584                        else:
1585                            download_progress = float(download_bytes) / size
1586                        if download_progress > next_progress_report:
1587                            next_progress_report += download_progress
1588                            if progress_callback(download_progress,\
1589                                   'Continuing download of data.',
1590                                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1591
1592                                file.close()
1593                                os.close(file_descriptor)
1594                                return None
1595            file.close()
1596            os.close(file_descriptor)
1597
1598        if progress_callback is not None:
1599            if progress_callback(0.4,
1600                                 'Data download complete. Reading data.',
1601                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1602                return None
1603
1604        return tmp_filename
1605
1606
1607    @staticmethod
1608    def read_data(
1609            filename: str,
1610            data_representation: DataRepresentation
1611        ) -> Union['spacepy.datamodel', 'xr.Dataset']:
1612        """
1613        Reads the data from the given file.
1614
1615        Parameters
1616        ----------
1617        filename
1618            Name of file to read.
1619        data_representation
1620            Requested data representation.
1621        Returns
1622        -------
1623        spacepy.datamodel or xr.Dataset
1624            Data from file.
1625        Raises
1626        ------
1627        Exception
1628            If an Exception is raise by either the spdm.fromCDF() or
1629            cdflib.cdf_to_xarray() functions.
1630        ModuleNotFoundError
1631            If the required spacepy.datamodel or the cdflib and xarray
1632            modules are not installed.
1633        """
1634        if data_representation is None:
1635            if SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1636                return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1637            if CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1638                return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1639                                     fillval_to_nan=True)
1640            raise ModuleNotFoundError(
1641                'neither the spacepy.datamodel nor the cdflib and '
1642                'xarray modules are installed')
1643
1644        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY and \
1645           not SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1646            raise ModuleNotFoundError('spacepy module must be installed')
1647        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY and \
1648           not CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1649            raise ModuleNotFoundError('cdflib and xarray modules must be installed')
1650
1651        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY:
1652            return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1653        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY:
1654            return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1655                                 fillval_to_nan=True)
1656        return None
1657
1658
1659    def get_data(
1660            self,
1661            dataset: str,
1662            variables: List[str],
1663            time0: Union[TimeInterval, List[TimeInterval], datetime, str],
1664            time1: Union[datetime, str] = None,
1665            **keywords: Union[
1666                Dict,
1667                DataRepresentation,
1668                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1669                Any]
1670        ) -> Tuple[Dict, 'spdm.SpaceData', 'xarray']:
1671        """
1672        Gets the specified data from the server.  The representation
1673        of the returned data is determined as follows:<br>
1674        1. If a dataRepresentation keyword parameter is given, its
1675           value will determine the representation of the returned
1676           data.  If no dataRepresenation keyword parameter is
1677           given, then<br>
1678        2. If the presence of spacepy.datamodel is found, then the data
1679           is returned in the spacepy.datamodel representation.<br>
1680        3. If the presence of the cdflib and xarray modules are found,
1681           then the data is returned in an xarray.Dataset.
1682
1683        Parameters
1684        ----------
1685        dataset
1686            dataset identifier of data to get.
1687        variables
1688            array containing names of variables to get.  The value
1689            ALL-VARIABLES may be used instead of specifying all the
1690            individual variable names.
1691        time0
1692            TimeInterval(s) or start time of data to get.  See module
1693            note about timezone.
1694        time1
1695            when time0 is not one or more TimeInterval(s), the end time
1696            of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1697        keywords
1698            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1699            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1700            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1701            the given binning parameter values.  See
1702            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/CDAWeb_Binning_readme.html>
1703            for more details.  binData is a Dict that
1704            may contain the following keys: interval,
1705            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1706            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1707            defaults.<br>
1708            <b>dataRepresentation</b> - specifies the representation of
1709            the returned data as one of
1710            `datarepresentation.DataRepresentation`.<br>
1711            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1712            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1713            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1714            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1715            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1716            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1717            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1718            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1719            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1720            the progress of this call.<br>
1721            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1722            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1723        Returns
1724        -------
1725        Tuple
1726            [0] contains a dictionary of HTTP and CDAS status information.
1727            When successful, ['http']['status_code'] will be 200.<br>
1728            [1] contains the requested data (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset
1729            object) or None.
1730        Raises
1731        ------
1732        ValueError
1733            If no variables are given or if the given start/end datetime
1734            values are invalid.
1735        """
1736        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1737        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1738        # pylint: disable=too-many-statements
1739        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1740        # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
1741
1742        #import spacepy.datamodel as spdm       # type: ignore
1743
1744        if len(variables) < 1:
1745            raise ValueError('at least one variable name is required')
1746
1747        if isinstance(time0, (str, datetime)):
1748            if isinstance(time1, (str, datetime)):
1749                time_intervals = [TimeInterval(time0, time1)]
1750            else:
1751                raise ValueError('time1 must be str/datetime')
1752        elif isinstance(time0, TimeInterval):
1753            time_intervals = [time0]
1754        elif isinstance(time0, list) and len(time0) > 0 and\
1755             isinstance(time0[0], TimeInterval):
1756            time_intervals = time0
1757        else:
1758            raise ValueError('invalid time0 type')
1759
1760        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1761                                  time_intervals,
1762                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1763                                  binData=keywords.get('binData', {}))
1764
1765        data_rep = keywords.get('dataRepresentation', None)
1766        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1767        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1768
1769        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1770
1771        status = {
1772            'http': {
1773                'status_code': 204
1774            },
1775            'cdas': {
1776                'status': [],
1777                'message': [],
1778                'warning': [],
1779                'error': []
1780            }
1781        }
1782
1783        if progress_callback is not None:
1784            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making initial server request.',
1785                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1786                return (status, None)
1787
1788        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1789                                                        progress_callback,
1790                                                        progress_user_value)
1791
1792        status['http']['status_code'] = status_code
1793
1794        if progress_callback is not None:
1795            if progress_callback(0.3, 'Initial server request complete.',
1796                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1797                return (status, None)
1798
1799        if status_code != 200:
1800
1801            self.logger.info('get_data_result failed with http code %d',
1802                             status_code)
1803            self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1804            return (status, None)
1805
1806        if not data_result:
1807            return (status, None)
1808
1809        if 'Status' in data_result:
1810            status['cdas']['status'] = data_result['Status']
1811        if 'Message' in data_result:
1812            status['cdas']['message'] = data_result['Message']
1813        if 'Warning' in data_result:
1814            status['cdas']['warning'] = data_result['Warning']
1815        if 'Error' in data_result:
1816            status['cdas']['error'] = data_result['Error']
1817
1818        if progress_callback is not None:
1819            if progress_callback(0.4, 'Beginning download of data.',
1820                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1821                return (status, None)
1822
1823        file_descriptions = data_result['FileDescription']
1824
1825        data_url = file_descriptions[0]['Name']
1826        data_length = file_descriptions[0]['Length']
1827
1828        self.logger.debug('data_url = %s, data_length = %d',
1829                          data_url, data_length)
1830
1831        sub_progress_control = {
1832            'progressCallback': progress_callback,
1833            'progressUserValue': progress_user_value,
1834            'progressStart': 0.4,
1835            'progressFraction': 0.1
1836        }
1837
1838        tmp_filename = self.download(data_url, data_length,
1839                                     progressCallback=_get_data_progress,
1840                                     progressUserValue=sub_progress_control)
1841
1842        try:
1843            data = self.read_data(tmp_filename, data_rep)
1844            os.remove(tmp_filename)
1845            if progress_callback is not None:
1846                if progress_callback(1.0, 'Finished reading data.',
1847                                     progress_user_value) != 0:
1848                    return (status, None)
1849        except:
1850            self.logger.error('Exception from read_data(%s): %s, %s',
1851                              tmp_filename, sys.exc_info()[0],
1852                              sys.exc_info()[1])
1853            self.logger.error('CDF file has been retained.')
1854            raise
1855        return (status, data)
1856
1857
1858    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
1859    def get_graph(
1860            self,
1861            dataset: str,
1862            variables: List[str],
1863            start: Union[datetime, str],
1864            end: Union[datetime, str],
1865            options: GraphOptions = None,
1866            image_format: List[ImageFormat] = None,
1867            **keywords
1868        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1869        """
1870        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1871        server.
1872
1873        Parameters
1874        ----------
1875        dataset
1876            dataset identifier of data to get.
1877        variables
1878            array containing names of variables to get.
1879        start
1880            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1881        end
1882            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1883        options
1884            graph options.
1885        image_format
1886            image format.  If None, then [ImageFormat.PNG].
1887        keywords
1888            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1889            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1890            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1891            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1892            may contain the following keys: interval,
1893            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1894            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1895            defaults.<br>
1896            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1897            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1898            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1899            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1900            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1901            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1902            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1903            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1904            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1905            the progress of this call.<br>
1906            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
1907            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1908        Returns
1909        -------
1910        Tuple
1911            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
1912            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
1913                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1914                DataResult object or None.<br>
1915        Raises
1916        ------
1917        ValueError
1918            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1919        """
1920        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1921        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1922        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1923        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1924
1925        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1926                                                                  end)
1927
1928        request = GraphRequest(dataset, variables,
1929                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
1930                               options, image_format,
1931                               **keywords)
1932
1933        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1934        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1935
1936        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
1937
1938        if progress_callback is not None:
1939            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1940                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1941                return (204, None)
1942
1943        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request, progress_callback, progress_user_value)
1944
1945        if progress_callback is not None:
1946            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
1947                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1948                return (status_code, None)
1949
1950        if status_code != 200:
1951
1952            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
1953                             status_code)
1954            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
1955            return (status_code, None)
1956
1957        return (status_code, result)
1958    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
1959
1960
1961    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
1962    def get_thumbnail(
1963            self,
1964            dataset: str,
1965            variables: List[str],
1966            start: Union[datetime, str],
1967            end: Union[datetime, str],
1968            identifier: str,
1969            thumbnail: int = 1,
1970            **keywords
1971        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1972        """
1973        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1974        server.
1975
1976        Parameters
1977        ----------
1978        dataset
1979            dataset identifier of data to get.
1980        variables
1981            array containing names of variables to get.
1982        start
1983            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1984        end
1985            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1986        identifier
1987            thumbnail identifier (returned in a previous get_graph
1988            result).
1989        thumbnail
1990            number of thumbnail whose full size image is being requested.
1991            Thumbnail images are counted beginning at one (not zero).
1992        keywords
1993            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1994            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1995            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1996            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1997            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1998            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1999            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2000            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2001            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2002            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2003            the progress of this call.<br>
2004            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2005            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2006        Returns
2007        -------
2008        Tuple
2009            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2010            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2011                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2012                DataResult object or None.<br>
2013        Raises
2014        ------
2015        ValueError
2016            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2017        """
2018        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2019        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2020        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2021        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2022
2023        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2024                                                                  end)
2025
2026        request = ThumbnailRequest(dataset, variables,
2027                                   TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2028                                   identifier, thumbnail)
2029
2030        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2031        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2032
2033        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2034
2035        if progress_callback is not None:
2036            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2037                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2038                return (204, None)
2039
2040        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2041                                                   progress_callback,
2042                                                   progress_user_value)
2043
2044        if progress_callback is not None:
2045            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2046                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2047                return (status_code, None)
2048
2049        if status_code != 200:
2050
2051            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2052                             status_code)
2053            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2054            return (status_code, None)
2055
2056        return (status_code, result)
2057    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
2058
2059
2060    # pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
2061    def get_text(
2062            self,
2063            dataset: str,
2064            variables: List[str],
2065            start: Union[datetime, str],
2066            end: Union[datetime, str],
2067            compression: Compression = Compression.UNCOMPRESSED,
2068            text_format: TextFormat = TextFormat.PLAIN,
2069            **keywords
2070        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2071        """
2072        Gets a textual representation of the specified data from the
2073        server.
2074
2075        Parameters
2076        ----------
2077        dataset
2078            dataset identifier of data to get.
2079        variables
2080            array containing names of variables to get.
2081        start
2082            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2083        end
2084            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2085        compression
2086            file compression.
2087        text_format
2088            text format.
2089        keywords
2090            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2091            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2092            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2093            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2094            may contain the following keys: interval,
2095            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2096            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2097            defaults.<br>
2098            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2099            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2100            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2101            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2102            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2103            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2104            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2105            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2106            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2107            the progress of this call.<br>
2108            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2109            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2110        Returns
2111        -------
2112        Tuple
2113            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2114            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2115                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2116                DataResult object or None.<br>
2117        Raises
2118        ------
2119        ValueError
2120            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2121        """
2122        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2123        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2124        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2125        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2126
2127        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2128                                                                  end)
2129
2130        request = TextRequest(dataset, variables,
2131                              TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2132                              compression, text_format,
2133                              **keywords)
2134
2135        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2136        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2137
2138        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2139
2140        if progress_callback is not None:
2141            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2142                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2143                return (204, None)
2144
2145        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2146                                                   progress_callback,
2147                                                   progress_user_value)
2148
2149        if progress_callback is not None:
2150            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2151                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2152                return (status_code, None)
2153
2154        if status_code != 200:
2155
2156            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2157                             status_code)
2158            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2159            return (status_code, None)
2160
2161        return (status_code, result)
2162    # pylint: enable=too-many-arguments
2163
2164
2165    def get_audio(
2166            self,
2167            dataset: str,
2168            variables: List[str],
2169            start: Union[datetime, str],
2170            end: Union[datetime, str],
2171            **keywords
2172        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2173        """
2174        Gets an audio representation of the specified data from the
2175        server.
2176
2177        Parameters
2178        ----------
2179        dataset
2180            dataset identifier of data to get.
2181        variables
2182            array containing names of variables to get.
2183        start
2184            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2185        end
2186            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2187        keywords
2188            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2189            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2190            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2191            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2192            may contain the following keys: interval,
2193            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2194            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2195            defaults.<br>
2196            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2197            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2198            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2199            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2200            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2201            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2202            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2203            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2204            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2205            the progress of this call.<br>
2206            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2207            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2208        Returns
2209        -------
2210        Tuple
2211            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2212            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2213                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2214                DataResult object or None.<br>
2215        Raises
2216        ------
2217        ValueError
2218            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2219        """
2220        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2221        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2222        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2223        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2224
2225        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2226                                                                  end)
2227
2228        request = AudioRequest(dataset, variables,
2229                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2230                               **keywords)
2231
2232        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2233        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2234
2235        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2236
2237        if progress_callback is not None:
2238            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2239                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2240                return (204, None)
2241
2242        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2243                                                   progress_callback,
2244                                                   progress_user_value)
2245
2246        if progress_callback is not None:
2247            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2248                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2249                return (status_code, None)
2250
2251        if status_code != 200:
2252
2253            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2254                             status_code)
2255            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2256            return (status_code, None)
2257
2258        return (status_code, result)
2259
2260
2261    def get_original_files(
2262            self,
2263            dataset: str,
2264            start: Union[datetime, str],
2265            end: Union[datetime, str],
2266            **keywords
2267        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2268        """
2269        Gets original data files from a dataset.  Original data files
2270        lack updated meta-data and virtual variable values contained
2271        in files obtained from the `CdasWs.get_data`.  Most callers
2272        should probably use `CdasWs.get_data` instead of this function.
2273
2274        Parameters
2275        ----------
2276        dataset
2277            dataset identifier of data to get.
2278        start
2279            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2280        end
2281            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2282        keywords
2283            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2284            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2285            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2286            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2287            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2288            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2289            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2290            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2291            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2292            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2293            the progress of this call.<br>
2294            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2295            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2296        Returns
2297        -------
2298        Tuple
2299            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2300            [1] array of dictionary representations of a
2301                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2302                FileDescription objects or None.<br>
2303        Raises
2304        ------
2305        ValueError
2306            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2307        See Also
2308        --------
2309        CdasWs.get_data
2310        """
2311        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2312        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2313        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2314        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2315
2316        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2317                                                                  end)
2318
2319        request = CdfRequest(dataset, [],
2320                             TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime))
2321
2322        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2323        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2324
2325        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2326
2327        if progress_callback is not None:
2328            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2329                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2330                return (204, None)
2331
2332        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2333                                                   progress_callback,
2334                                                   progress_user_value)
2335
2336        if progress_callback is not None:
2337            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2338                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2339                return (status_code, None)
2340
2341        if status_code != 200:
2342
2343            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2344                             status_code)
2345            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2346            return (status_code, None)
2347
2348        return (status_code, result['FileDescription'])
2349
2350
2351    def get_ssc_id(
2352            self,
2353            dataset: str
2354        ) -> Tuple[int, Union[str, List[str]]]:
2355        """
2356        Gets the Satellite Situation Center (SSC)
2357        <https://sscweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/> observatory identifier(s)
2358        associated with the given cdaweb dataset identifier.
2359
2360        Notes
2361        -----
2362        This method relies upon the Heliophysics Data Portal's
2363        <https://heliophysicsdata.gsfc.nasa.gov/> metadata.  That metadata
2364        may be incomplete.  Also, cdaweb has datasets for which SSC has
2365        no corresponding observatory (for example, ground observatory
2366        data).  Callers should be prepared for negative results (200, None)
2367        from this method.
2368
2369        Parameters
2370        ----------
2371        dataset
2372            cdaweb dataset identifier.
2373        Returns
2374        -------
2375        Tuple
2376            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2377            [1] the SSC observatory identifier(s) associated with the given
2378                cdaweb dataset identifier or None if none is found.
2379        """
2380        url = self._hdp_registry + '?cdawebId=' + dataset
2381
2382        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
2383
2384        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
2385
2386        if response.status_code != 200:
2387
2388            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
2389                             response.status_code)
2390            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
2391            return (response.status_code, None)
2392
2393        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
2394            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
2395
2396        results = ET.fromstring(response.text)
2397
2398        ssc_id = []
2399        for ssc_id_elem in results.findall('SscId'):
2400            ssc_id.append(ssc_id_elem.text)
2401
2402        if len(ssc_id) == 0:
2403            result = None
2404        elif len(ssc_id) == 1:
2405            result = ssc_id[0]
2406        else:
2407            result = ssc_id
2408
2409        return (response.status_code, result)

Class representing the web service interface to NASA's Coordinated Data Analysis System (CDAS) https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov.

Notes

The logger used by this class has the class' name (CdasWs). By default, it is configured with a NullHandler. Users of this class may configure the logger to aid in diagnosing problems.

CdasWs( endpoint=None, timeout=None, proxy=None, ca_certs=None, disable_ssl_certificate_validation=False, user_agent=None, disable_cache=False) 
211    def __init__(
212            self,
213            endpoint=None,
214            timeout=None,
215            proxy=None,
216            ca_certs=None,
217            disable_ssl_certificate_validation=False,
218            user_agent=None,
219            disable_cache=False):
220        """
221        Creates an object representing the CDAS web services.
222
223        Parameters
224        ----------
225        endpoint
226            URL of the CDAS web service.  If None, the default is
227            'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'.
228        timeout
229            Number of seconds to wait for a response from the server.
230        proxy
231            HTTP proxy information.  For example,<pre>
232            proxies = {
233              'http': 'http://10.10.1.10:3128',
234              'https': 'http://10.10.1.10:1080',
235            }</pre>
236            Proxy information can also be set with environment variables.
237            For example,<pre>
238            $ export HTTP_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:3128"
239            $ export HTTPS_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:1080"</pre>
240        ca_certs
241            Path to certificate authority (CA) certificates that will
242            override the default bundle.
243        disable_ssl_certificate_validation
244            Flag indicating whether to validate the SSL certificate.
245        user_agent
246            A value that is appended to the HTTP User-Agent value.
247        disable_cache
248            Flag indicating whether to disable HTTP caching.
249        """
250
251        self.logger = logging.getLogger(type(self).__name__)
252        self.logger.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
253
254        self.logger.debug('endpoint = %s', endpoint)
255        self.logger.debug('ca_certs = %s', ca_certs)
256        self.logger.debug('disable_ssl_certificate_validation = %s',
257                          disable_ssl_certificate_validation)
258        self.logger.debug('disable_cache = %s', disable_cache)
259
260        if endpoint is None:
261            self._endpoint = 'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'
262        else:
263            self._endpoint = endpoint
264
265        self._user_agent = 'cdasws/' + __version__ + ' (' + \
266            platform.python_implementation() + ' ' \
267            + platform.python_version() + '; ' + platform.platform() + ')'
268
269        if user_agent is not None:
270            self._user_agent += ' (' + user_agent + ')'
271
272        self._request_headers = {
273            #'Content-Type' : 'application/json',
274            'Content-Type' : 'application/xml',
275            'Accept' : 'application/xml',
276            'User-Agent' : self._user_agent,
277            #'Accept-Encoding' : 'gzip'  # only beneficial for icdfml responses
278        }
279        if CACHE_AVAILABLE and disable_cache is not True:
280            self._session = requests_cache.CachedSession('cdasws_cache',
281                                                         cache_control=True)
282        else:
283            self._session = requests.Session()
284
285        self._session.headers.update(self._request_headers)
286        self._session.auth = NullAuth()
287
288        if ca_certs is not None:
289            self._session.verify = ca_certs
290
291        if disable_ssl_certificate_validation is True:
292            self._session.verify = False
293
294        if proxy is not None:
295            self._proxy = proxy
296
297        self._timeout = timeout
298
299        endpoint_components = urlparse(self._endpoint)
300        self._hdp_registry = endpoint_components.scheme + '://' + \
301            endpoint_components.netloc + '/registry/hdp/SscId.xql'

Creates an object representing the CDAS web services.

Parameters
  • endpoint: URL of the CDAS web service. If None, the default is 'https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/'.
  • timeout: Number of seconds to wait for a response from the server.
  • proxy: HTTP proxy information. For example,
    proxies = {
'http': 'http://10.10.1.10:3128',
'https': 'http://10.10.1.10:1080',
}
    Proxy information can also be set with environment variables. For example,
    $ export HTTP_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:3128"
$ export HTTPS_PROXY="http://10.10.1.10:1080"
  • ca_certs: Path to certificate authority (CA) certificates that will override the default bundle.
  • disable_ssl_certificate_validation: Flag indicating whether to validate the SSL certificate.
  • user_agent: A value that is appended to the HTTP User-Agent value.
  • disable_cache: Flag indicating whether to disable HTTP caching.
logger
def close(self) -> None: 
314    def close(self) -> None:
315        """
316        Closes any persistent network connections.  Generally, deleting
317        this object is sufficient and calling this method is unnecessary.
318        """
319        self._session.close()

Closes any persistent network connections. Generally, deleting this object is sufficient and calling this method is unnecessary.

def get_observatory_groups(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
322    def get_observatory_groups(
323            self,
324            **keywords: str
325        ) -> List[Dict]:
326        """
327        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups from the server.
328
329        Parameters
330        ----------
331        keywords
332            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
333            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
334            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting
335            this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
336            based upon their instrumentType value.
337        Returns
338        -------
339        List
340            An array of ObservatoryGroupDescription
341            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
342            ObservatoryGroupDescription in
343            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
344        """
345        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
346            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups?instrumentType=' + \
347                      urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
348        else:
349            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroups'
350
351        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
352
353        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
354
355        if response.status_code != 200:
356
357            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
358                             response.status_code)
359            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
360            return []
361
362        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
363            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
364
365        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
366
367        observatory_group_descriptions = []
368        for description in observatory_response.findall(\
369            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupDescription', namespaces=NS):
370
371            observatory_ids = []
372            for observatory_id in description.findall(\
373                'cdas:ObservatoryId', namespaces=NS):
374
375                observatory_ids.append(observatory_id.text)
376
377            observatory_group_descriptions.append({
378                'Name': description.find(\
379                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
380                'ObservatoryId': observatory_ids
381            })
382
383        return observatory_group_descriptions

Gets descriptions of the observatory groups from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    instrumentType - an instrument type value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instrument_types. Omitting this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon their instrumentType value.
Returns
  • List: An array of ObservatoryGroupDescription dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors ObservatoryGroupDescription in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_instrument_types(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
386    def get_instrument_types(
387            self,
388            **keywords: str
389        ) -> List[Dict]:
390        """
391        Gets descriptions of the instrument types from the server.
392
393        Parameters
394        ----------
395        keywords
396            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
397            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
398            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
399            indicates that no instrumentTypes are eliminated based upon
400            their observatory value.<br>
401            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from
402            those returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting
403            this parameter indicates that no instrumentTypes are
404            eliminated based upon their observatoryGroup value.</br>
405        Returns
406        -------
407        List
408            An array of InstrumentTypeDescription
409            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
410            InstrumentTypeDescription in
411            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
412        """
413        if 'observatory' in keywords:
414            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatory=' \
415                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
416        elif 'observatoryGroup' in keywords:
417            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes?observatoryGroup=' \
418                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatoryGroup'])
419        else:
420            url = self._endpoint + 'instrumentTypes'
421
422        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
423
424        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
425
426        if response.status_code != 200:
427
428            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
429                             response.status_code)
430            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
431            return []
432
433        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
434            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
435
436        instrument_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
437
438        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
439            self.logger.debug('instrument_response = %s',
440                              ET.tostring(instrument_response))
441
442        instrument_types = []
443        for description in instrument_response.findall(\
444            'cdas:InstrumentTypeDescription', namespaces=NS):
445
446            instrument_types.append({
447                'Name': description.find('cdas:Name',
448                                         namespaces=NS).text
449                })
450        return instrument_types

Gets descriptions of the instrument types from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    observatory - an observatory value from those returned by CdasWs.get_observatories. Omitting this parameter indicates that no instrumentTypes are eliminated based upon their observatory value.
    observatoryGroup - an observatory group value from those returned by CdasWs.get_observatory_groups. Omitting this parameter indicates that no instrumentTypes are eliminated based upon their observatoryGroup value.
Returns
  • List: An array of InstrumentTypeDescription dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors InstrumentTypeDescription in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_instruments(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
453    def get_instruments(
454            self,
455            **keywords: str
456        ) -> List[Dict]:
457        """
458        Gets descriptions of the instruments from the server.
459
460        Parameters
461        ----------
462        keywords
463            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
464            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory value from those returned
465            by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this parameter
466            indicates that no instruments are eliminated based upon their
467            observatory value.<br>
468            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
469            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
470            parameter indicates that no instruments are eliminated based
471            upon their instrument type.<br>
472        Returns
473        -------
474        List
475            An array of InstrumentDescription
476            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
477            InstrumentDescription in
478            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
479        """
480        if 'observatory' in keywords:
481            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?observatory=' \
482                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['observatory'])
483        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
484            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments?instrumentType=' \
485                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
486        else:
487            url = self._endpoint + 'instruments'
488
489        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
490
491        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
492
493        if response.status_code != 200:
494
495            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
496                             response.status_code)
497            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
498            return []
499
500        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
501            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
502
503        instruments_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
504
505        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
506            self.logger.debug('instruments = %s', response.text)
507                              #ET.indent(instruments_response, space='    '))
508
509        instruments = []
510        for instrument_description in instruments_response.findall(\
511            'cdas:InstrumentDescription', namespaces=NS):
512
513            instruments.append({
514                'Name': instrument_description.find(\
515                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
516                'ShortDescription': instrument_description.find(\
517                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
518                'LongDescription': instrument_description.find(\
519                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
520            })
521
522        return instruments

Gets descriptions of the instruments from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    observatory - an observatory value from those returned by CdasWs.get_observatories. Omitting this parameter indicates that no instruments are eliminated based upon their observatory value.
    instrumentType - an instrument type value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instrument_types. Omitting this parameter indicates that no instruments are eliminated based upon their instrument type.
Returns
  • List: An array of InstrumentDescription dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors InstrumentDescription in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_observatories(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
525    def get_observatories(
526            self,
527            **keywords: str
528        ) -> List[Dict]:
529        """
530        Gets descriptions of the observatories from the server.
531
532        Parameters
533        ----------
534        keywords
535            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
536            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned
537            by `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter
538            indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon
539            their instrument value.<br>
540            <b>instrumentType</b> - in instrument type value from those
541            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
542            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
543            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
544        Returns
545        -------
546        List
547            An array of ObservatoryDescriptions
548            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
549            ObservatoryDescription in
550            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
551        """
552        if 'instrument' in keywords:
553            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrument=' \
554                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrument'])
555        elif 'instrumentType' in keywords:
556            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories?instrumentType=' \
557                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
558        else:
559            url = self._endpoint + 'observatories'
560
561        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
562
563        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
564
565        if response.status_code != 200:
566
567            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
568                             response.status_code)
569            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
570            return []
571
572        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
573            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
574
575        observatory_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
576
577        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
578            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
579
580        observatories = []
581
582        for observatory in observatory_response.findall(\
583            'cdas:ObservatoryDescription', namespaces=NS):
584            observatories.append({
585                'Name': observatory.find(\
586                    'cdas:Name', namespaces=NS).text,
587                'ShortDescription': observatory.find(\
588                    'cdas:ShortDescription', namespaces=NS).text,
589                'LongDescription': observatory.find(\
590                    'cdas:LongDescription', namespaces=NS).text
591            })
592
593        return observatories

Gets descriptions of the observatories from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    instrument - an instrument value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instruments. Omitting this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon their instrument value.
    instrumentType - in instrument type value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instrument_types. Omitting this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon their instrumentType value.
Returns
  • List: An array of ObservatoryDescriptions dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors ObservatoryDescription in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_observatory_groups_and_instruments(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
596    def get_observatory_groups_and_instruments(
597            self,
598            **keywords: str
599        ) -> List[Dict]:
600        """
601        Gets descriptions of the observatory groups (and associated
602        instruments) from the server.
603
604        Parameters
605        ----------
606        keywords
607            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
608            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
609            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
610            parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated
611            based upon their instrumentType value.<br>
612        Returns
613        -------
614        List
615            An array of ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription
616            dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors
617            ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription in
618            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
619        """
620        if 'instrumentType' in keywords:
621            url = self._endpoint \
622                  + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments?instrumentType=' \
623                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['instrumentType'])
624        else:
625            url = self._endpoint + 'observatoryGroupsAndInstruments'
626
627        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
628
629        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
630
631        if response.status_code != 200:
632
633            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
634                             response.status_code)
635            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
636            return []
637
638        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
639            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
640
641        observatories_response = ET.fromstring(response.text)
642
643        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
644            self.logger.debug('observatories = %s', response.text)
645
646        o_g_i_ds = []
647
648        for o_g_i_d in observatories_response.findall(\
649            'cdas:ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription',\
650            namespaces=NS):
651
652            o_g_i_d_name = o_g_i_d.find('cdas:Name',
653                                        namespaces=NS).text
654            o_is = []
655            for o_i in o_g_i_d.findall('cdas:ObservatoryInstruments',
656                                       namespaces=NS):
657
658                o_i_name = o_i.find('cdas:Name',
659                                    namespaces=NS).text
660                i_ds = []
661                for i_d in o_i.findall('cdas:InstrumentDescription',
662                                       namespaces=NS):
663                    i_d_name = i_d.find('cdas:Name',
664                                        namespaces=NS).text
665                    i_d_short_description = \
666                        i_d.find('cdas:ShortDescription',
667                                 namespaces=NS).text
668                    i_d_long_description = \
669                         i_d.find('cdas:LongDescription',
670                                  namespaces=NS).text
671                    i_ds.append({
672                        'Name': i_d_name,
673                        'ShortDescription': i_d_short_description,
674                        'LongDescription': i_d_long_description
675                    })
676                o_is.append({
677                    'Name': o_i_name,
678                    'InstrumentDescription': i_ds
679                })
680
681            o_g_i_ds.append({
682                'Name': o_g_i_d_name,
683                'ObservatoryInstruments': o_is
684            })
685
686        return o_g_i_ds

Gets descriptions of the observatory groups (and associated instruments) from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    instrumentType - an instrument type value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instrument_types. Omitting this parameter indicates that no observatories are eliminated based upon their instrumentType value.
Returns
  • List: An array of ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription dictionaries where the structure of the dictionary mirrors ObservatoryGroupInstrumentDescription in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_datasets(self, **keywords: str) -> List[Dict]: 
690    def get_datasets(
691            self,
692            **keywords: str
693        ) -> List[Dict]:
694        """
695        Gets descriptions of the specified datasets from the server.
696
697        Parameters
698        ----------
699        keywords
700            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
701            <b>observatoryGroup</b> - an observatory group value from those
702            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatory_groups`.  Omitting this
703            parameter
704            indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their
705            observatoryGroup value.<br>
706            <b>instrumentType</b> - an instrument type value from those
707            returned by `CdasWs.get_instrument_types`.  Omitting this
708            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
709            upon their instrumentType value.<br>
710            <b>observatory</b> - an observatory name value from those
711            returned by `CdasWs.get_observatories`.  Omitting this
712            parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based
713            upon their observatory value.<br>
714            <b>instrument</b> - an instrument value from those returned by
715            `CdasWs.get_instruments`.  Omitting this parameter indicates
716            that no datasets are eliminated based upon their instrument
717            value.<br>
718            <b>startDate</b> - a datetime specifying the start of a time
719            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
720            parameter is ommited, the time interval will begin infinitely
721            in the past.<br>
722            <b>stopDate</b> - a datetime specifying the end of a time
723            interval.  See module note about timezone value.  If this
724            parameter is omitted, the time interval will end infinitely
725            in the future.<br>
726            <b>id</b> - a dataset identifier. The value may be a CDAS
727            (e.g., AC_H2_MFI), DOI (e.g., 10.48322/fh85-fj47), or SPASE
728            [ResourceID] (e.g., spase://NASA/NumericalData/ACE/MAG/L2/PT1H)
729            identifier. If specified, all other keywords are ignored.<br>
730            <b>idPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
731            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS identifier value.
732            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.<br>
733            <b>labelPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
734            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS label text.
735            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
736            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?i)` for case insensitive
737            match mode) are supported and likely to be useful in this
738            case.<br>
739            <b>notesPattern</b> - a java.util.regex compatible regular
740            expression that must match the dataset's CDAS notes text.
741            Omitting this parameter is equivalent to `.*`.  Embedded
742            matching flag expressions (e.g., `(?s)` for dotall match mode)
743            are supported and likely to be useful in this case.<br>
744        Returns
745        -------
746        List
747            A list of dictionaries containing descriptions of the datasets
748            requested.  The dictionary structure is defined by the
749            DatasetDescription element in
750            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
751        """
752        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets?'
753
754        observatory_groups = keywords.get('observatoryGroup', None)
755        if observatory_groups is not None:
756            if isinstance(observatory_groups, str):
757                observatory_groups = [observatory_groups]
758            for observatory_group in observatory_groups:
759                url = url + 'observatoryGroup=' \
760                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory_group) + '&'
761
762        instrument_types = keywords.get('instrumentType', None)
763        if instrument_types is not None:
764            if isinstance(instrument_types, str):
765                instrument_types = [instrument_types]
766            for instrument_type in instrument_types:
767                url = url + 'instrumentType=' \
768                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument_type) + '&'
769
770        observatories = keywords.get('observatory', None)
771        if observatories is not None:
772            if isinstance(observatories, str):
773                observatories = [observatories]
774            for observatory in observatories:
775                url = url + 'observatory=' \
776                      + urllib.parse.quote(observatory) + '&'
777
778        instruments = keywords.get('instrument', None)
779        if instruments is not None:
780            if isinstance(instruments, str):
781                instruments = [instruments]
782            for instrument in instruments:
783                url = url + 'instrument=' \
784                      + urllib.parse.quote(instrument) + '&'
785
786        if 'startDate' in keywords:
787            url = url + 'startDate=' \
788                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['startDate']) + '&'
789
790        if 'stopDate' in keywords:
791            url = url + 'stopDate=' \
792                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['stopDate']) + '&'
793
794        if 'id' in keywords:
795            url = url + 'id=' \
796                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['id']) + '&'
797
798        if 'idPattern' in keywords:
799            url = url + 'idPattern=' \
800                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['idPattern']) + '&'
801
802        if 'labelPattern' in keywords:
803            url = url + 'labelPattern=' \
804                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['labelPattern']) + '&'
805
806        if 'notesPattern' in keywords:
807            url = url + 'notesPattern=' \
808                  + urllib.parse.quote(keywords['notesPattern']) + '&'
809
810        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url[:-1])
811
812        response = self._session.get(url[:-1], timeout=self._timeout)
813
814        if response.status_code != 200:
815
816            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
817                             response.status_code)
818            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
819            return []
820
821        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
822            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
823
824        dss = ET.fromstring(response.text)
825
826        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
827            self.logger.debug('datasets = %s', response.text)
828
829        datasets = []
830        for ds in dss.findall('cdas:DatasetDescription',
831                              namespaces=NS):
832
833            observatory_groups = []
834            for o_g in ds.findall('cdas:ObservatoryGroup',
835                                  namespaces=NS):
836                observatory_groups.append(o_g.text)
837
838            instrument_types = []
839            for i_t in ds.findall('cdas:InstrumentType',
840                                  namespaces=NS):
841                instrument_types.append(i_t.text)
842
843            dataset_links = []
844            for d_l in ds.findall('cdas:DatasetLink',
845                                  namespaces=NS):
846                dataset_links.append({
847                    'Title': d_l.find('cdas:Title',
848                                      namespaces=NS).text,
849                    'Text': d_l.find('cdas:Text',
850                                     namespaces=NS).text,
851                    'Url': d_l.find('cdas:Url',
852                                    namespaces=NS).text,
853                })
854
855            observatories = []
856            for obs_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Observatory',
857                                       namespaces=NS):
858                observatories.append(obs_elem.text)
859
860            instruments = []
861            for instr_elem in ds.findall('cdas:Instrument',
862                                         namespaces=NS):
863                instruments.append(instr_elem.text)
864
865            dataset = {
866                'Id': ds.find('cdas:Id', namespaces=NS).text,
867                'Observatory': observatories,
868                'Instrument': instruments,
869                'ObservatoryGroup': observatory_groups,
870                'InstrumentType': instrument_types,
871                'Label': ds.find('cdas:Label',
872                                 namespaces=NS).text,
873                'TimeInterval': {
874                    'Start': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:Start',
875                                     namespaces=NS).text,
876                    'End': ds.find('cdas:TimeInterval/cdas:End',
877                                   namespaces=NS).text
878                },
879                'PiName': ds.find('cdas:PiName',
880                                  namespaces=NS).text,
881                'PiAffiliation': ds.find('cdas:PiAffiliation',
882                                         namespaces=NS).text,
883                'Notes': ds.find('cdas:Notes',
884                                 namespaces=NS).text,
885                'DatasetLink': dataset_links
886            }
887            doi = ds.find('cdas:Doi', namespaces=NS)
888            if doi is not None:
889                dataset['Doi'] = doi.text
890
891            spase_resource_id = ds.find('cdas:SpaseResourceId',
892                                        namespaces=NS)
893            if spase_resource_id is not None:
894                dataset['SpaseResourceId'] = spase_resource_id.text
895
896            additional_metadata = []
897            for add_meta in ds.findall('cdas:AdditionalMetadata',
898                                       namespaces=NS):
899                meta_type = add_meta.attrib['Type']
900                value = add_meta.text
901                additional_metadata.append({
902                    'Type': meta_type,
903                    'value': value
904                })
905
906            if len(additional_metadata) > 0:
907                dataset['AdditionalMetadata'] = additional_metadata
908
909            datasets.append(dataset)
910
911        return sorted(datasets, key=itemgetter('Id'))

Gets descriptions of the specified datasets from the server.

Parameters
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    observatoryGroup - an observatory group value from those returned by CdasWs.get_observatory_groups. Omitting this parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their observatoryGroup value.
    instrumentType - an instrument type value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instrument_types. Omitting this parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their instrumentType value.
    observatory - an observatory name value from those returned by CdasWs.get_observatories. Omitting this parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their observatory value.
    instrument - an instrument value from those returned by CdasWs.get_instruments. Omitting this parameter indicates that no datasets are eliminated based upon their instrument value.
    startDate - a datetime specifying the start of a time interval. See module note about timezone value. If this parameter is ommited, the time interval will begin infinitely in the past.
    stopDate - a datetime specifying the end of a time interval. See module note about timezone value. If this parameter is omitted, the time interval will end infinitely in the future.
    id - a dataset identifier. The value may be a CDAS (e.g., AC_H2_MFI), DOI (e.g., 10.48322/fh85-fj47), or SPASE [ResourceID] (e.g., spase://NASA/NumericalData/ACE/MAG/L2/PT1H) identifier. If specified, all other keywords are ignored.
    idPattern - a java.util.regex compatible regular expression that must match the dataset's CDAS identifier value. Omitting this parameter is equivalent to .*.
    labelPattern - a java.util.regex compatible regular expression that must match the dataset's CDAS label text. Omitting this parameter is equivalent to .*. Embedded matching flag expressions (e.g., (?i) for case insensitive match mode) are supported and likely to be useful in this case.
    notesPattern - a java.util.regex compatible regular expression that must match the dataset's CDAS notes text. Omitting this parameter is equivalent to .*. Embedded matching flag expressions (e.g., (?s) for dotall match mode) are supported and likely to be useful in this case.
Returns
  • List: A list of dictionaries containing descriptions of the datasets requested. The dictionary structure is defined by the DatasetDescription element in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_client_library_example(self, dataset: str, language: str = 'Python') -> str: 
915    def get_client_library_example(
916            self,
917            dataset: str,
918            language: str = 'Python'
919        ) -> str:
920        """
921        Gets client library example code for the given dataset and
922        programming language.
923
924        Parameters
925        ----------
926        dataset
927            The dataset to use in the example code.  It must be an Id
928            returned by get_datasets.
929        language
930            The programming language of the example code.  It must be
931            either Python or IDL.
932
933        Returns
934        -------
935        str
936            Requested client library example code.
937        """
938        library = 'cdaswsPy'
939        if language.lower() == 'idl':
940            library = 'cdaswsIdl'
941
942        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
943                dataset + '/clientLibraryExample/' + library
944
945        # Do not use session headers (Accept: Application/xml)
946        response = requests.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
947
948        if response.status_code != 200:
949            self.logger.warning('%s failed with status %d', response.url,
950                                response.status_code)
951            return None
952
953        return response.text

Gets client library example code for the given dataset and programming language.

Parameters
  • dataset: The dataset to use in the example code. It must be an Id returned by get_datasets.
  • language: The programming language of the example code. It must be either Python or IDL.
Returns
  • str: Requested client library example code.
@staticmethod
def get_doi_landing_page_url(doi: str) -> str: 
956    @staticmethod
957    def get_doi_landing_page_url(
958            doi: str
959        ) -> str:
960        """
961        Returns a URL to the given Digital Object Identifier's landing
962        page (metadata for the DOI).
963
964        Note: this method is deprecated.  You should call 
965        doi.get_doi_landing_page_url directly.
966
967        Parameters
968        ----------
969        doi
970            digital object identifier.
971        Returns
972        -------
973        str
974            A URL to the DOI's landing page.
975        """
976
977        return get_doi_landing_page_url(doi)

Returns a URL to the given Digital Object Identifier's landing page (metadata for the DOI).

Note: this method is deprecated. You should call doi.get_doi_landing_page_url directly.

Parameters
  • doi: digital object identifier.
Returns
  • str: A URL to the DOI's landing page.
@staticmethod
def get_citation(doi: str) -> str: 
 980    @staticmethod
 981    def get_citation(
 982            doi: str
 983        ) -> str:
 984        """
 985        Returns the citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 986
 987        Parameters
 988        ----------
 989        doi
 990            digital object identifier.
 991        Returns
 992        -------
 993        str
 994            The citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
 995        """
 996
 997        url = 'https://doi.org/' + doi
 998        headers = {'Accept': 'text/x-bibliography; style=apa'}
 999        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers,
1000                                timeout=30)
1001
1002        return response.text

Returns the citation from doi.org for the given DOI.

Parameters
  • doi: digital object identifier.
Returns
  • str: The citation from doi.org for the given DOI.
def get_inventory( self, identifier: str, **keywords: str) -> List[cdasws.timeinterval.TimeInterval]: 
1005    def get_inventory(
1006            self,
1007            identifier: str,
1008            **keywords: str
1009        ) -> List[TimeInterval]:
1010        """
1011        Gets a description of the specified dataset's data inventory.
1012
1013        Parameters
1014        ----------
1015        identifier
1016            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1017        keywords
1018            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1019            <b>timeInterval</b> - `timeinterval.TimeInterval` to restrict
1020            returned inventory.
1021        Returns
1022        -------
1023        List
1024            An array of `timeinterval.TimeInterval`s when data is
1025            available.
1026        """
1027
1028        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1029                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/inventory'
1030
1031        if 'timeInterval' in keywords:
1032            time_interval_keyword = keywords['timeInterval']
1033            url = url + '/' + \
1034                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.start) + \
1035                  ',' + \
1036                  TimeInterval.basic_iso_format(time_interval_keyword.end)
1037
1038        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
1039
1040        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1041
1042        if response.status_code != 200:
1043
1044            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
