1045                             response.status_code)
1046            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1047            return []
1048
1049        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1050            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1051
1052        inventory = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1053        intervals = []
1054        for inventory_desc in inventory.findall(\
1055                                  'cdas:InventoryDescription',
1056                                  namespaces=NS):
1057            for time_interval in inventory_desc.findall(\
1058                                     'cdas:TimeInterval',
1059                                     namespaces=NS):
1060                intervals.append(
1061                    TimeInterval(
1062                        time_interval.find('cdas:Start',
1063                                           namespaces=NS).text,
1064                        time_interval.find('cdas:End',
1065                                           namespaces=NS).text
1066                    )
1067                )
1068
1069        return intervals

Gets a description of the specified dataset's data inventory.

Parameters
  • identifier: dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    timeInterval - timeinterval.TimeInterval to restrict returned inventory.
Returns
  • List: An array of timeinterval.TimeIntervals when data is available.
def get_example_time_interval(self, identifier: str) -> cdasws.timeinterval.TimeInterval: 
1072    def get_example_time_interval(
1073            self,
1074            identifier: str,
1075        ) -> TimeInterval:
1076        """
1077        Gets a small example time interval for the specified dataset.  The
1078        interval is near the end of the dataset's data inventory.  The
1079        returned interval is not guaranteed to have non-fill data for any
1080        specific variable.
1081
1082        Parameters
1083        ----------
1084        identifier
1085            dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
1086        Returns
1087        -------
1088        timeinterval.TimeInterval
1089            An small example time interval that is likely, but not
1090            guaranteed, to have data or None if an interval cannot be
1091            found.
1092        """
1093
1094        time_intervals = self.get_inventory(identifier)
1095        if len(time_intervals) < 1:
1096            return None
1097        example_interval = time_intervals[-1]
1098        if re.search('MMS[1-4]_.+_BRST_.+', identifier):
1099            time_delta = timedelta(seconds=1)
1100        else:
1101            time_delta = timedelta(hours=2)
1102        example_interval.start = example_interval.end - time_delta
1103        return example_interval

Gets a small example time interval for the specified dataset. The interval is near the end of the dataset's data inventory. The returned interval is not guaranteed to have non-fill data for any specific variable.

Parameters
  • identifier: dataset identifier of data inventory to get.
Returns
  • timeinterval.TimeInterval: An small example time interval that is likely, but not guaranteed, to have data or None if an interval cannot be found.
def get_variables(self, identifier: str) -> List[Dict]: 
1106    def get_variables(
1107            self,
1108            identifier: str
1109        ) -> List[Dict]:
1110        """
1111        Gets a description of the variables in the specified dataset.
1112
1113        Parameters
1114        ----------
1115        identifier
1116            dataset identifier of data to get.
1117        Returns
1118        -------
1119        List
1120            A List of dictionary descriptions of the variables in
1121            the specified dataset.  The dictionary structure is defined by
1122            the VariableDescription element in
1123            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>.
1124        """
1125
1126        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets/' + \
1127                  urllib.parse.quote(identifier, safe='') + '/variables'
1128
1129        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
1130
1131        if response.status_code != 200:
1132
1133            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1134                             response.status_code)
1135            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1136            return []
1137
1138        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
1139            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
1140
1141        var_descriptions = ET.fromstring(response.text)
1142
1143        variables = []
1144        for var_description in var_descriptions.findall(\
1145                                   'cdas:VariableDescription',
1146                                   namespaces=NS):
1147            name = var_description.find('cdas:Name',
1148                                        namespaces=NS).text
1149            short_description = var_description.find(\
1150                                        'cdas:ShortDescription',
1151                                        namespaces=NS).text
1152            if short_description is None:
1153                short_description = ''
1154
1155            long_description = var_description.find(\
1156                                        'cdas:LongDescription',
1157                                        namespaces=NS).text
1158            if long_description is None:
1159                long_description = ''
1160
1161            variables.append({
1162                'Name': name,
1163                'ShortDescription': short_description,
1164                'LongDescription': long_description
1165            })
1166
1167        return variables

Gets a description of the variables in the specified dataset.

Parameters
  • identifier: dataset identifier of data to get.
Returns
  • List: A List of dictionary descriptions of the variables in the specified dataset. The dictionary structure is defined by the VariableDescription element in https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd.
def get_variable_names(self, identifier: str) -> List[str]: 
1170    def get_variable_names(
1171            self,
1172            identifier: str
1173        ) -> List[str]:
1174        """
1175        Gets the names of the variables in the specified dataset.  This
1176        method is like the get_variables method except that it only returns
1177        the variable names and not the other metadata.
1178
1179        Parameters
1180        ----------
1181        identifier
1182            dataset identifier of data to get.
1183        Returns
1184        -------
1185        List
1186            A List of the names of the variables in the specified dataset.
1187        """
1188
1189        variable_names = []
1190        for variable in self.get_variables(identifier):
1191            variable_names.append(variable['Name'])
1192
1193        return variable_names

Gets the names of the variables in the specified dataset. This method is like the get_variables method except that it only returns the variable names and not the other metadata.

Parameters
  • identifier: dataset identifier of data to get.
Returns
  • List: A List of the names of the variables in the specified dataset.
def get_data_result( self, data_request: cdasws.datarequest.DataRequest, progress_callback: Callable[[float, str, Any], int], progress_user_value: Any) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
1333    def get_data_result(
1334            self,
1335            data_request: DataRequest,
1336            progress_callback: Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1337            progress_user_value: Any
1338        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1339        """
1340        Submits the given request to the server and returns the result.
1341        This is a relatively low-level method and most callers should
1342        probably use a higher-level method such as get_data.
1343
1344        Parameters
1345        ----------
1346        data_request
1347            data request.
1348        progress_callback
1349            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1350            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1351            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1352            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1353            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1354            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1355            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1356            the progress of this call.
1357        progressUserValue
1358            value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
1359        Returns
1360        -------
1361        Tuple
1362            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1363            successful.<br>
1364            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1365            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1366            or None.
1367        See Also
1368        --------
1369        CdasWs.get_data
1370        """
1371
1372        #self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.json())
1373        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request.xml_str())
1374
1375        url = self._endpoint + 'datasets'
1376
1377        for retries in range(RETRY_LIMIT):
1378            #response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.json(),
1379            response = self._session.post(url, data=data_request.xml_str(),
1380                                          timeout=self._timeout)
1381
1382            if response.status_code == 200:
1383
1384                data_result = CdasWs._get_data_result_dict(response.text)
1385
1386                if not data_result:
1387                    return (response.status_code, None)
1388
1389                return (response.status_code, data_result)
1390
1391            if response.status_code == 429 or \
1392               response.status_code == 503 and \
1393               'Retry-After' in response.headers:
1394
1395                retry_after = response.headers['Retry-After']
1396
1397                self.logger.debug('429/503 status with Retry-After header: %s',
1398                                  retry_after)
1399
1400                if progress_callback is not None:
1401                    if progress_callback(0.2, 'Waiting ' + retry_after + \
1402                                         's before making server request.',
1403                                         progress_user_value) != 0:
1404                        return (204, None)
1405
1406                retry_after = int(retry_after)
1407
1408                self.logger.info('Sleeping %d seconds before making request',
1409                                 retry_after)
1410                time.sleep(retry_after)
1411
1412            else:
1413                self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
1414                                 response.status_code)
1415                self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1416                self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1417                return (response.status_code, None)
1418
1419        self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d after %d retries',
1420                         url, response.status_code, retries + 1)
1421        self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1422        self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
1423        return (response.status_code, None)

Submits the given request to the server and returns the result. This is a relatively low-level method and most callers should probably use a higher-level method such as get_data.

Parameters
  • data_request: data request.
  • progress_callback: function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns a non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
  • progressUserValue: value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the int HTTP status code. 200 when successful.
    [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd or None.
See Also

CdasWs.get_data

def get_data_file( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], **keywords: Union[Dict, Callable[[float, str, Any], int], Any]) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
1426    def get_data_file(
1427            self,
1428            dataset: str,
1429            variables: List[str],
1430            start: Union[datetime, str], end: Union[datetime, str],
1431            **keywords: Union[
1432                Dict,
1433                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1434                Any]
1435        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1436        """
1437        Gets the specified data file from the server.
1438
1439        Parameters
1440        ----------
1441        dataset
1442            dataset identifier of data to get.
1443        variables
1444            array containing names of variables to get.
1445        start
1446            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1447        end
1448            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1449        keywords
1450            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1451            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1452            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1453            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1454            may contain the following keys: interval,
1455            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1456            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1457            defaults.<br>
1458            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1459            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1460            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1461            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1462            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1463            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data_file()
1464            function will immediately return (204, None).  The float
1465            parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress
1466            and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1467            the progress of this call.<br>
1468            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1469            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1470        Returns
1471        -------
1472        Tuple
1473            [0] contains the int HTTP status code.  200 when
1474            successful.<br>
1475            [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from
1476            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1477            or None.
1478        Raises
1479        ------
1480        ValueError
1481            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1482        See Also
1483        --------
1484        CdasWs.get_data : In addition to what get_data_file does,
1485            get_data also downloads and reads the data file into memory
1486            (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset object).
1487        """
1488        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1489        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1490        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1491        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1492
1493        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1494                                                                  end)
1495
1496        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1497                                  TimeInterval(start_datetime,
1498                                               end_datetime),
1499                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1500                                  **keywords.get('binData', {}))
1501
1502        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1503        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1504
1505        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1506
1507        if progress_callback is not None:
1508            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1509                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1510                return (204, None)
1511
1512        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1513                                                        progress_callback,
1514                                                        progress_user_value)
1515
1516        if progress_callback is not None:
1517            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Initial server request complete.',
1518                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1519                return (status_code, None)
1520
1521        return (status_code, data_result)

Gets the specified data file from the server.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    binData - indicates that uniformly spaced values should be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to the given binning parameter values. binData is a Dict that may contain the following keys: interval, interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the defaults.
    progressCallback - is a Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data_file() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is an Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the int HTTP status code. 200 when successful.
    [1] contains a dictionary representing the DataResult from https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
See Also

CdasWs.get_data: In addition to what get_data_file does, get_data also downloads and reads the data file into memory (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset object).

def download(self, url: str, size: int = 0, **keywords) -> str: 
1524    def download(
1525            self,
1526            url: str,
1527            size: int = 0,
1528            **keywords
1529        ) -> str:
1530        """
1531        Downloads the file specified by the given URL to a temporary
1532        file without reading all of it into memory.  This method
1533        utilizes the connection pool and persistent HTTP connection
1534        to the CdasWs server.
1535
1536        Parameters
1537        ----------
1538        url
1539            URL of file to download.
1540        size
1541            number of bytes in file to download.
1542        keywords
1543            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1544            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1545            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1546            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1547            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1548            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns a non-0 value,
1549            getting the data will be aborted and this download() function
1550            will immediately return None.  The float parameter
1551            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1552            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1553            the progress of this call.<br>
1554            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
1555            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1556        Returns
1557        -------
1558        str
1559            name of tempory file or None if there was an error.
1560        """
1561        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1562
1563        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1564        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1565
1566        suffix = os.path.splitext(urlparse(url).path)[1]
1567
1568        file_descriptor, tmp_filename = mkstemp(suffix=suffix)
1569
1570        download_bytes = 0
1571        next_progress_report = 0.1
1572        with self._session.get(url, stream=True,
1573                               timeout=self._timeout) as response:
1574
1575            file = open(tmp_filename, 'wb')
1576            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
1577                if chunk:  # filter out keep-alive new chunks
1578                    file.write(chunk)
1579                    # file.flush()
1580                    if progress_callback is not None:
1581                        download_bytes += len(chunk)
1582                        if size == 0:
1583                            download_progress = 0.0
1584                        else:
1585                            download_progress = float(download_bytes) / size
1586                        if download_progress > next_progress_report:
1587                            next_progress_report += download_progress
1588                            if progress_callback(download_progress,\
1589                                   'Continuing download of data.',
1590                                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1591
1592                                file.close()
1593                                os.close(file_descriptor)
1594                                return None
1595            file.close()
1596            os.close(file_descriptor)
1597
1598        if progress_callback is not None:
1599            if progress_callback(0.4,
1600                                 'Data download complete. Reading data.',
1601                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1602                return None
1603
1604        return tmp_filename

Downloads the file specified by the given URL to a temporary file without reading all of it into memory. This method utilizes the connection pool and persistent HTTP connection to the CdasWs server.

Parameters
  • url: URL of file to download.
  • size: number of bytes in file to download.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns a non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and this download() function will immediately return None. The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • str: name of tempory file or None if there was an error.
@staticmethod
def read_data( filename: str, data_representation: cdasws.datarepresentation.DataRepresentation) -> Union[ForwardRef('spacepy.datamodel'), ForwardRef('xr.Dataset')]: 
1607    @staticmethod
1608    def read_data(
1609            filename: str,
1610            data_representation: DataRepresentation
1611        ) -> Union['spacepy.datamodel', 'xr.Dataset']:
1612        """
1613        Reads the data from the given file.
1614
1615        Parameters
1616        ----------
1617        filename
1618            Name of file to read.
1619        data_representation
1620            Requested data representation.
1621        Returns
1622        -------
1623        spacepy.datamodel or xr.Dataset
1624            Data from file.
1625        Raises
1626        ------
1627        Exception
1628            If an Exception is raise by either the spdm.fromCDF() or
1629            cdflib.cdf_to_xarray() functions.
1630        ModuleNotFoundError
1631            If the required spacepy.datamodel or the cdflib and xarray
1632            modules are not installed.
1633        """
1634        if data_representation is None:
1635            if SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1636                return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1637            if CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1638                return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1639                                     fillval_to_nan=True)
1640            raise ModuleNotFoundError(
1641                'neither the spacepy.datamodel nor the cdflib and '
1642                'xarray modules are installed')
1643
1644        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY and \
1645           not SPDM_AVAILABLE:
1646            raise ModuleNotFoundError('spacepy module must be installed')
1647        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY and \
1648           not CDF_XARRAY_AVAILABLE:
1649            raise ModuleNotFoundError('cdflib and xarray modules must be installed')
1650
1651        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.SPACEPY:
1652            return spdm.fromCDF(filename)
1653        if data_representation is DataRepresentation.XARRAY:
1654            return cdf_to_xarray(filename, to_datetime=True,
1655                                 fillval_to_nan=True)
1656        return None

Reads the data from the given file.

Parameters
  • filename: Name of file to read.
  • data_representation: Requested data representation.
Returns
  • spacepy.datamodel or xr.Dataset: Data from file.
Raises
  • Exception: If an Exception is raise by either the spdm.fromCDF() or cdflib.cdf_to_xarray() functions.
  • ModuleNotFoundError: If the required spacepy.datamodel or the cdflib and xarray modules are not installed.
def get_data( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], time0: Union[cdasws.timeinterval.TimeInterval, List[cdasws.timeinterval.TimeInterval], datetime.datetime, str], time1: Union[datetime.datetime, str] = None, **keywords: Union[Dict, cdasws.datarepresentation.DataRepresentation, Callable[[float, str, Any], int], Any]) -> Tuple[Dict, spacepy.datamodel.SpaceData, <module 'xarray' from '/home/btharris/opt/python/39/cdasws/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/xarray/__init__.py'>]: 
1659    def get_data(
1660            self,
1661            dataset: str,
1662            variables: List[str],
1663            time0: Union[TimeInterval, List[TimeInterval], datetime, str],
1664            time1: Union[datetime, str] = None,
1665            **keywords: Union[
1666                Dict,
1667                DataRepresentation,
1668                Callable[[float, str, Any], int],
1669                Any]
1670        ) -> Tuple[Dict, 'spdm.SpaceData', 'xarray']:
1671        """
1672        Gets the specified data from the server.  The representation
1673        of the returned data is determined as follows:<br>
1674        1. If a dataRepresentation keyword parameter is given, its
1675           value will determine the representation of the returned
1676           data.  If no dataRepresenation keyword parameter is
1677           given, then<br>
1678        2. If the presence of spacepy.datamodel is found, then the data
1679           is returned in the spacepy.datamodel representation.<br>
1680        3. If the presence of the cdflib and xarray modules are found,
1681           then the data is returned in an xarray.Dataset.
1682
1683        Parameters
1684        ----------
1685        dataset
1686            dataset identifier of data to get.
1687        variables
1688            array containing names of variables to get.  The value
1689            ALL-VARIABLES may be used instead of specifying all the
1690            individual variable names.
1691        time0
1692            TimeInterval(s) or start time of data to get.  See module
1693            note about timezone.
1694        time1
1695            when time0 is not one or more TimeInterval(s), the end time
1696            of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1697        keywords
1698            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1699            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1700            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1701            the given binning parameter values.  See
1702            <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/CDAWeb_Binning_readme.html>
1703            for more details.  binData is a Dict that
1704            may contain the following keys: interval,
1705            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1706            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1707            defaults.<br>
1708            <b>dataRepresentation</b> - specifies the representation of
1709            the returned data as one of
1710            `datarepresentation.DataRepresentation`.<br>
1711            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1712            Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1713            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1714            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1715            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1716            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1717            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1718            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1719            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1720            the progress of this call.<br>
1721            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is an Any value that is passsed
1722            to the progressCallback function.<br>
1723        Returns
1724        -------
1725        Tuple
1726            [0] contains a dictionary of HTTP and CDAS status information.
1727            When successful, ['http']['status_code'] will be 200.<br>
1728            [1] contains the requested data (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset
1729            object) or None.
1730        Raises
1731        ------
1732        ValueError
1733            If no variables are given or if the given start/end datetime
1734            values are invalid.
1735        """
1736        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1737        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1738        # pylint: disable=too-many-statements
1739        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1740        # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
1741
1742        #import spacepy.datamodel as spdm       # type: ignore
1743
1744        if len(variables) < 1:
1745            raise ValueError('at least one variable name is required')
1746
1747        if isinstance(time0, (str, datetime)):
1748            if isinstance(time1, (str, datetime)):
1749                time_intervals = [TimeInterval(time0, time1)]
1750            else:
1751                raise ValueError('time1 must be str/datetime')
1752        elif isinstance(time0, TimeInterval):
1753            time_intervals = [time0]
1754        elif isinstance(time0, list) and len(time0) > 0 and\
1755             isinstance(time0[0], TimeInterval):
1756            time_intervals = time0
1757        else:
1758            raise ValueError('invalid time0 type')
1759
1760        data_request = CdfRequest(dataset, variables,
1761                                  time_intervals,
1762                                  3, CdfFormat.BINARY,
1763                                  binData=keywords.get('binData', {}))
1764
1765        data_rep = keywords.get('dataRepresentation', None)
1766        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1767        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1768
1769        self.logger.debug('data_request = %s', data_request)
1770
1771        status = {
1772            'http': {
1773                'status_code': 204
1774            },
1775            'cdas': {
1776                'status': [],
1777                'message': [],
1778                'warning': [],
1779                'error': []
1780            }
1781        }
1782
1783        if progress_callback is not None:
1784            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making initial server request.',
1785                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1786                return (status, None)
1787
1788        status_code, data_result = self.get_data_result(data_request,
1789                                                        progress_callback,
1790                                                        progress_user_value)
1791
1792        status['http']['status_code'] = status_code
1793
1794        if progress_callback is not None:
1795            if progress_callback(0.3, 'Initial server request complete.',
1796                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1797                return (status, None)
1798
1799        if status_code != 200:
1800
1801            self.logger.info('get_data_result failed with http code %d',
1802                             status_code)
1803            self.logger.info('data_request = %s', data_request)
1804            return (status, None)
1805
1806        if not data_result:
1807            return (status, None)
1808
1809        if 'Status' in data_result:
1810            status['cdas']['status'] = data_result['Status']
1811        if 'Message' in data_result:
1812            status['cdas']['message'] = data_result['Message']
1813        if 'Warning' in data_result:
1814            status['cdas']['warning'] = data_result['Warning']
1815        if 'Error' in data_result:
1816            status['cdas']['error'] = data_result['Error']
1817
1818        if progress_callback is not None:
1819            if progress_callback(0.4, 'Beginning download of data.',
1820                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1821                return (status, None)
1822
1823        file_descriptions = data_result['FileDescription']
1824
1825        data_url = file_descriptions[0]['Name']
1826        data_length = file_descriptions[0]['Length']
1827
1828        self.logger.debug('data_url = %s, data_length = %d',
1829                          data_url, data_length)
1830
1831        sub_progress_control = {
1832            'progressCallback': progress_callback,
1833            'progressUserValue': progress_user_value,
1834            'progressStart': 0.4,
1835            'progressFraction': 0.1
1836        }
1837
1838        tmp_filename = self.download(data_url, data_length,
1839                                     progressCallback=_get_data_progress,
1840                                     progressUserValue=sub_progress_control)
1841
1842        try:
1843            data = self.read_data(tmp_filename, data_rep)
1844            os.remove(tmp_filename)
1845            if progress_callback is not None:
1846                if progress_callback(1.0, 'Finished reading data.',
1847                                     progress_user_value) != 0:
1848                    return (status, None)
1849        except:
1850            self.logger.error('Exception from read_data(%s): %s, %s',
1851                              tmp_filename, sys.exc_info()[0],
1852                              sys.exc_info()[1])
1853            self.logger.error('CDF file has been retained.')
1854            raise
1855        return (status, data)

Gets the specified data from the server. The representation of the returned data is determined as follows:

  1. If a dataRepresentation keyword parameter is given, its value will determine the representation of the returned data. If no dataRepresenation keyword parameter is given, then
  2. If the presence of spacepy.datamodel is found, then the data is returned in the spacepy.datamodel representation.
  3. If the presence of the cdflib and xarray modules are found, then the data is returned in an xarray.Dataset.
Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get. The value ALL-VARIABLES may be used instead of specifying all the individual variable names.
  • time0: TimeInterval(s) or start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • time1: when time0 is not one or more TimeInterval(s), the end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    binData - indicates that uniformly spaced values should be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to the given binning parameter values. See https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/CDAWeb_Binning_readme.html for more details. binData is a Dict that may contain the following keys: interval, interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the defaults.
    dataRepresentation - specifies the representation of the returned data as one of datarepresentation.DataRepresentation.
    progressCallback - is a Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is an Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains a dictionary of HTTP and CDAS status information. When successful, ['http']['status_code'] will be 200.
    [1] contains the requested data (SpaceData or xarray.Dataset object) or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If no variables are given or if the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
def get_graph( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], options: cdasws.datarequest.GraphOptions = None, image_format: List[cdasws.datarequest.ImageFormat] = None, **keywords) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
1859    def get_graph(
1860            self,
1861            dataset: str,
1862            variables: List[str],
1863            start: Union[datetime, str],
1864            end: Union[datetime, str],
1865            options: GraphOptions = None,
1866            image_format: List[ImageFormat] = None,
1867            **keywords
1868        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1869        """
1870        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1871        server.
1872
1873        Parameters
1874        ----------
1875        dataset
1876            dataset identifier of data to get.
1877        variables
1878            array containing names of variables to get.
1879        start
1880            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1881        end
1882            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1883        options
1884            graph options.
1885        image_format
1886            image format.  If None, then [ImageFormat.PNG].
1887        keywords
1888            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1889            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
1890            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
1891            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
1892            may contain the following keys: interval,
1893            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
1894            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
1895            defaults.<br>
1896            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1897            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1898            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1899            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1900            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1901            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
1902            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
1903            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
1904            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
1905            the progress of this call.<br>
1906            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
1907            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
1908        Returns
1909        -------
1910        Tuple
1911            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
1912            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
1913                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
1914                DataResult object or None.<br>
1915        Raises
1916        ------
1917        ValueError
1918            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
1919        """
1920        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
1921        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
1922        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
1923        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
1924
1925        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
1926                                                                  end)
1927
1928        request = GraphRequest(dataset, variables,
1929                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
1930                               options, image_format,
1931                               **keywords)
1932
1933        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
1934        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
1935
1936        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
1937
1938        if progress_callback is not None:
1939            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
1940                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1941                return (204, None)
1942
1943        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request, progress_callback, progress_user_value)
1944
1945        if progress_callback is not None:
1946            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
1947                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
1948                return (status_code, None)
1949
1950        if status_code != 200:
1951
1952            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
1953                             status_code)
1954            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
1955            return (status_code, None)
1956
1957        return (status_code, result)

Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the server.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • options: graph options.
  • image_format: image format. If None, then [ImageFormat.PNG].
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    binData - indicates that uniformly spaced values should be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to the given binning parameter values. binData is a Dict that may contain the following keys: interval, interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the defaults.
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] contains a dictionary representation of a https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd DataResult object or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
def get_thumbnail( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], identifier: str, thumbnail: int = 1, **keywords) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
1962    def get_thumbnail(
1963            self,
1964            dataset: str,
1965            variables: List[str],
1966            start: Union[datetime, str],
1967            end: Union[datetime, str],
1968            identifier: str,
1969            thumbnail: int = 1,
1970            **keywords
1971        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
1972        """
1973        Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the
1974        server.
1975
1976        Parameters
1977        ----------
1978        dataset
1979            dataset identifier of data to get.
1980        variables
1981            array containing names of variables to get.
1982        start
1983            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1984        end
1985            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
1986        identifier
1987            thumbnail identifier (returned in a previous get_graph
1988            result).
1989        thumbnail
1990            number of thumbnail whose full size image is being requested.
1991            Thumbnail images are counted beginning at one (not zero).
1992        keywords
1993            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
1994            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
1995            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
1996            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
1997            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
1998            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
1999            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2000            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2001            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2002            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2003            the progress of this call.<br>
2004            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2005            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2006        Returns
2007        -------
2008        Tuple
2009            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2010            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2011                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2012                DataResult object or None.<br>
2013        Raises
2014        ------
2015        ValueError
2016            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2017        """
2018        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2019        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2020        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2021        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2022
2023        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2024                                                                  end)
2025
2026        request = ThumbnailRequest(dataset, variables,
2027                                   TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2028                                   identifier, thumbnail)
2029
2030        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2031        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2032
2033        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2034
2035        if progress_callback is not None:
2036            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2037                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2038                return (204, None)
2039
2040        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2041                                                   progress_callback,
2042                                                   progress_user_value)
2043
2044        if progress_callback is not None:
2045            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2046                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2047                return (status_code, None)
2048
2049        if status_code != 200:
2050
2051            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2052                             status_code)
2053            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2054            return (status_code, None)
2055
2056        return (status_code, result)

Gets a graphical representation of the specified data from the server.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • identifier: thumbnail identifier (returned in a previous get_graph result).
  • thumbnail: number of thumbnail whose full size image is being requested. Thumbnail images are counted beginning at one (not zero).
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] contains a dictionary representation of a https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd DataResult object or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
def get_text( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], compression: cdasws.datarequest.Compression = <Compression.UNCOMPRESSED: 'Uncompressed'>, text_format: cdasws.datarequest.TextFormat = <TextFormat.PLAIN: 'Plain'>, **keywords) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
2061    def get_text(
2062            self,
2063            dataset: str,
2064            variables: List[str],
2065            start: Union[datetime, str],
2066            end: Union[datetime, str],
2067            compression: Compression = Compression.UNCOMPRESSED,
2068            text_format: TextFormat = TextFormat.PLAIN,
2069            **keywords
2070        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2071        """
2072        Gets a textual representation of the specified data from the
2073        server.
2074
2075        Parameters
2076        ----------
2077        dataset
2078            dataset identifier of data to get.
2079        variables
2080            array containing names of variables to get.
2081        start
2082            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2083        end
2084            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2085        compression
2086            file compression.
2087        text_format
2088            text format.
2089        keywords
2090            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2091            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2092            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2093            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2094            may contain the following keys: interval,
2095            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2096            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2097            defaults.<br>
2098            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2099            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2100            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2101            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2102            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2103            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2104            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2105            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2106            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2107            the progress of this call.<br>
2108            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2109            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2110        Returns
2111        -------
2112        Tuple
2113            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2114            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2115                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2116                DataResult object or None.<br>
2117        Raises
2118        ------
2119        ValueError
2120            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2121        """
2122        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2123        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2124        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2125        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2126
2127        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2128                                                                  end)
2129
2130        request = TextRequest(dataset, variables,
2131                              TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2132                              compression, text_format,
2133                              **keywords)
2134
2135        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2136        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2137
2138        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2139
2140        if progress_callback is not None:
2141            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2142                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2143                return (204, None)
2144
2145        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2146                                                   progress_callback,
2147                                                   progress_user_value)
2148
2149        if progress_callback is not None:
2150            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2151                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2152                return (status_code, None)
2153
2154        if status_code != 200:
2155
2156            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2157                             status_code)
2158            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2159            return (status_code, None)
2160
2161        return (status_code, result)

Gets a textual representation of the specified data from the server.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • compression: file compression.
  • text_format: text format.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    binData - indicates that uniformly spaced values should be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to the given binning parameter values. binData is a Dict that may contain the following keys: interval, interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the defaults.
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] contains a dictionary representation of a https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd DataResult object or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
def get_audio( self, dataset: str, variables: List[str], start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], **keywords) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
2165    def get_audio(
2166            self,
2167            dataset: str,
2168            variables: List[str],
2169            start: Union[datetime, str],
2170            end: Union[datetime, str],
2171            **keywords
2172        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2173        """
2174        Gets an audio representation of the specified data from the
2175        server.
2176
2177        Parameters
2178        ----------
2179        dataset
2180            dataset identifier of data to get.
2181        variables
2182            array containing names of variables to get.
2183        start
2184            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2185        end
2186            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2187        keywords
2188            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2189            <b>binData</b> - indicates that uniformly spaced values should
2190            be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to
2191            the given binning parameter values.  binData is a Dict that
2192            may contain the following keys: interval,
2193            interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or
2194            overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the
2195            defaults.<br>
2196            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2197            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2198            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2199            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2200            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2201            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2202            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2203            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2204            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2205            the progress of this call.<br>
2206            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2207            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2208        Returns
2209        -------
2210        Tuple
2211            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2212            [1] contains a dictionary representation of a
2213                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2214                DataResult object or None.<br>
2215        Raises
2216        ------
2217        ValueError
2218            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2219        """
2220        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2221        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2222        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2223        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2224
2225        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2226                                                                  end)
2227
2228        request = AudioRequest(dataset, variables,
2229                               TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime),
2230                               **keywords)
2231
2232        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2233        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2234
2235        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2236
2237        if progress_callback is not None:
2238            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2239                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2240                return (204, None)
2241
2242        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2243                                                   progress_callback,
2244                                                   progress_user_value)
2245
2246        if progress_callback is not None:
2247            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2248                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2249                return (status_code, None)
2250
2251        if status_code != 200:
2252
2253            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2254                             status_code)
2255            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2256            return (status_code, None)
2257
2258        return (status_code, result)

Gets an audio representation of the specified data from the server.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • variables: array containing names of variables to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    binData - indicates that uniformly spaced values should be computed for scaler/vector/spectrogram data according to the given binning parameter values. binData is a Dict that may contain the following keys: interval, interpolateMissingValues, sigmaMultiplier, and/or overrideDefaultBinning with values that override the defaults.
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] contains a dictionary representation of a https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd DataResult object or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
def get_original_files( self, dataset: str, start: Union[datetime.datetime, str], end: Union[datetime.datetime, str], **keywords) -> Tuple[int, Dict]: 
2261    def get_original_files(
2262            self,
2263            dataset: str,
2264            start: Union[datetime, str],
2265            end: Union[datetime, str],
2266            **keywords
2267        ) -> Tuple[int, Dict]:
2268        """
2269        Gets original data files from a dataset.  Original data files
2270        lack updated meta-data and virtual variable values contained
2271        in files obtained from the `CdasWs.get_data`.  Most callers
2272        should probably use `CdasWs.get_data` instead of this function.
2273
2274        Parameters
2275        ----------
2276        dataset
2277            dataset identifier of data to get.
2278        start
2279            start time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2280        end
2281            end time of data to get.  See module note about timezone.
2282        keywords
2283            optional keyword parameters as follows:<br>
2284            <b>progressCallback</b> - is a
2285            typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int]
2286            function that is called repeatedly to report the progress
2287            of getting the data.  The function should return 0 if it
2288            wants to continue getting data.  If it returns non-0 value,
2289            getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function
2290            will immediately return (204, None).  The float parameter
2291            is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and
2292            the str parameter will contain a text message indicating
2293            the progress of this call.<br>
2294            <b>progressUserValue</b> - is a typing.Any value that is
2295            passsed to the progressCallback function.<br>
2296        Returns
2297        -------
2298        Tuple
2299            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2300            [1] array of dictionary representations of a
2301                <https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd>
2302                FileDescription objects or None.<br>
2303        Raises
2304        ------
2305        ValueError
2306            If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
2307        See Also
2308        --------
2309        CdasWs.get_data
2310        """
2311        # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
2312        # pylint: disable=too-many-return-statements
2313        # pylint: enable=too-many-statements
2314        # pylint: disable=too-many-branches
2315
2316        start_datetime, end_datetime = TimeInterval.get_datetimes(start,
2317                                                                  end)
2318
2319        request = CdfRequest(dataset, [],
2320                             TimeInterval(start_datetime, end_datetime))
2321
2322        progress_callback = keywords.get('progressCallback', None)
2323        progress_user_value = keywords.get('progressUserValue', None)
2324
2325        self.logger.debug('request = %s', request)
2326
2327        if progress_callback is not None:
2328            if progress_callback(0.1, 'Making server request.',
2329                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2330                return (204, None)
2331
2332        status_code, result = self.get_data_result(request,
2333                                                   progress_callback,
2334                                                   progress_user_value)
2335
2336        if progress_callback is not None:
2337            if progress_callback(1.0, 'Server request complete.',
2338                                 progress_user_value) != 0:
2339                return (status_code, None)
2340
2341        if status_code != 200:
2342
2343            self.logger.info('get_result failed with http code %d',
2344                             status_code)
2345            self.logger.info('request = %s', request)
2346            return (status_code, None)
2347
2348        return (status_code, result['FileDescription'])

Gets original data files from a dataset. Original data files lack updated meta-data and virtual variable values contained in files obtained from the CdasWs.get_data. Most callers should probably use CdasWs.get_data instead of this function.

Parameters
  • dataset: dataset identifier of data to get.
  • start: start time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • end: end time of data to get. See module note about timezone.
  • keywords: optional keyword parameters as follows:
    progressCallback - is a typing.Callable[[float, str, typing.Any], int] function that is called repeatedly to report the progress of getting the data. The function should return 0 if it wants to continue getting data. If it returns non-0 value, getting the data will be aborted and the get_data() function will immediately return (204, None). The float parameter is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 to indicate progress and the str parameter will contain a text message indicating the progress of this call.
    progressUserValue - is a typing.Any value that is passsed to the progressCallback function.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] array of dictionary representations of a https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WebServices/REST/CDAS.xsd FileDescription objects or None.
Raises
  • ValueError: If the given start/end datetime values are invalid.
See Also

CdasWs.get_data

def get_ssc_id(self, dataset: str) -> Tuple[int, Union[str, List[str]]]: 
2351    def get_ssc_id(
2352            self,
2353            dataset: str
2354        ) -> Tuple[int, Union[str, List[str]]]:
2355        """
2356        Gets the Satellite Situation Center (SSC)
2357        <https://sscweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/> observatory identifier(s)
2358        associated with the given cdaweb dataset identifier.
2359
2360        Notes
2361        -----
2362        This method relies upon the Heliophysics Data Portal's
2363        <https://heliophysicsdata.gsfc.nasa.gov/> metadata.  That metadata
2364        may be incomplete.  Also, cdaweb has datasets for which SSC has
2365        no corresponding observatory (for example, ground observatory
2366        data).  Callers should be prepared for negative results (200, None)
2367        from this method.
2368
2369        Parameters
2370        ----------
2371        dataset
2372            cdaweb dataset identifier.
2373        Returns
2374        -------
2375        Tuple
2376            [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).<br>
2377            [1] the SSC observatory identifier(s) associated with the given
2378                cdaweb dataset identifier or None if none is found.
2379        """
2380        url = self._hdp_registry + '?cdawebId=' + dataset
2381
2382        self.logger.debug('request url = %s', url)
2383
2384        response = self._session.get(url, timeout=self._timeout)
2385
2386        if response.status_code != 200:
2387
2388            self.logger.info('%s failed with http code %d', url,
2389                             response.status_code)
2390            self.logger.info('response.text: %s', response.text)
2391            return (response.status_code, None)
2392
2393        if self.logger.level <= logging.DEBUG:
2394            self.logger.debug('response.text = %s', response.text)
2395
2396        results = ET.fromstring(response.text)
2397
2398        ssc_id = []
2399        for ssc_id_elem in results.findall('SscId'):
2400            ssc_id.append(ssc_id_elem.text)
2401
2402        if len(ssc_id) == 0:
2403            result = None
2404        elif len(ssc_id) == 1:
2405            result = ssc_id[0]
2406        else:
2407            result = ssc_id
2408
2409        return (response.status_code, result)

Gets the Satellite Situation Center (SSC) https://sscweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/ observatory identifier(s) associated with the given cdaweb dataset identifier.

Notes

This method relies upon the Heliophysics Data Portal's https://heliophysicsdata.gsfc.nasa.gov/ metadata. That metadata may be incomplete. Also, cdaweb has datasets for which SSC has no corresponding observatory (for example, ground observatory data). Callers should be prepared for negative results (200, None) from this method.

Parameters
  • dataset: cdaweb dataset identifier.
Returns
  • Tuple: [0] contains the HTTP status code value (200 when successful).
    [1] the SSC observatory identifier(s) associated with the given cdaweb dataset identifier or None if none is found.
